Please only trade with funds you can afford to lose, and consider consulting a financial professional before deploying real capital.\n\nThat said, building a crypto trading agent is a legitimate and interesting engineering challenge. The core components are:\n\n- **Market data ingestion**: connecting to exchange APIs (Binance, Coinbase, etc.) or DEX data sources (on-chain data, DEX aggregator APIs) to get real-time and historical price/volume data.\n- **Strategy engine**: the logic that decides when to buy or sell. This can range from simple (moving average crossovers, RSI thresholds) to complex (ML-based prediction, arbitrage detection, sentiment analysis).\n- **Execution layer**: the code that actually submits orders \u2014 either via centralized exchange APIs or on-chain transactions for DEXs.\n- **Portfolio manager**: tracks your holdings, calculates P&L, manages position sizing, and enforces risk limits (stop-losses, max drawdown, max position size).\n- **Logging and monitoring**: you need full visibility into every decision the agent makes and why.\n\n**Phase 2 \u2014 Paper trading and backtesting**\n\nRun your strategies against historical data and in paper-trading mode for weeks or months. If you've never used one before, that's totally fine \u2014 but you will need to get comfortable with it for a project like this, since that's where you run Python code, install packages, and manage your project.\n\n**Claude Code** is Anthropic's tool that runs inside that terminal. Instead of chatting here in the browser, you'd type `claude` in your terminal and have a conversation with Claude right there \u2014 but Claude can also see your project files, create new ones, run your code, and fix errors directly. It's powerful for building software.\n\n**That said**, given where you're at, here's what I'd honestly suggest as your learning path before jumping into building a trading bot:\n\n1. **Get comfortable with the terminal** \u2014 learn to navigate folders, run commands\n2. **Learn some Python basics** \u2014 variables, functions, loops, libraries\n3. **Install and try Claude Code** \u2014 use it to help you learn by building small things first\n4. **Then** come back to the trading agent project with those foundations in place\n\nA trading bot touches a lot of complex topics at once \u2014 APIs, async programming, financial math, blockchain transactions, risk management.       "file_text": "\"\"\"\nConfiguration system for the trading agent.\n\nLoading priority (each layer overrides the previous):\n  1. configs/config.default.yaml   \u2014 shipped defaults\n  2. configs/config.local.yaml     \u2014 your personal overrides (git-ignored)\n  3. Environment variables          \u2014 prefixed AGENT_ (e.g. AGENT_MODE=live)\n  4. .env file                      \u2014 loaded via python-dotenv\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Literal\n\nimport yaml\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Project paths\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\nPROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]  # two levels up from src/agent/\nCONFIGS_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / \"configs\"\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Config sub-models\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\nclass ExchangeConfig(BaseModel):\n    name: str = \"binance\"\n    sandbox: bool = True\n    rate_limit_ms: int = 1200\n\n\nclass TradingConfig(BaseModel):\n    pairs: list[str] = Field(default_factory=lambda: [\"BTC/USDT\", \"ETH/USDT\"])\n    base_currency: str = \"USDT\"\n    default_timeframe: str = \"1h\"\n\n\nclass StrategyConfig(BaseModel):\n    name: str = \"sma_crossover\"\n    params: dict = Field(default_factory=dict)\n\n\nclass RiskConfig(BaseModel):\n    max_position_pct: float = 5.0\n    max_open_positions: int = 3\n    stop_loss_pct: float = 3.0\n    take_profit_pct: float = 6.0\n    max_daily_loss_pct: float = 10.0\n\n    @field_validator(\"max_position_pct\", \"stop_loss_pct\", \"take_profit_pct\", \"max_daily_loss_pct\")\n    @classmethod\n    def must_be_positive(cls, v: float) -> float:\n        if v <= 0:\n            raise ValueError(\"Percentage values must be > 0\")\n        return v\n\n\nclass PaperConfig(BaseModel):\n    starting_balance: float = 10_000.0\n\n\nclass LoggingConfig(BaseModel):\n    level: Literal[\"DEBUG\", \"INFO\", \"WARNING\", \"ERROR\"] = \"INFO\"\n    format: Literal[\"console\", \"json\"] = \"console\"\n    log_file: str = \"logs/agent.log\"\n\n\nclass SchedulerConfig(BaseModel):\n    strategy_interval_seconds: int = 60\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Root config\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\nclass AgentConfig(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"Top-level configuration \u2014 the single source of truth for the agent.\"\"\"\n\n    mode: Literal[\"paper\", \"live\"] = \"paper\"\n    exchange: ExchangeConfig = Field(default_factory=ExchangeConfig)\n    trading: TradingConfig = Field(default_factory=TradingConfig)\n    strategy: StrategyConfig = Field(default_factory=StrategyConfig)\n    risk: RiskConfig = Field(default_factory=RiskConfig)\n    paper: PaperConfig = Field(default_factory=PaperConfig)\n    logging: LoggingConfig = Field(default_factory=LoggingConfig)\n    scheduler: SchedulerConfig = Field(default_factory=SchedulerConfig)\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Loader\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\ndef _deep_merge(base: dict, override: dict) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Recursively merge *override* into *base* (mutates base).\"\"\"\n    for key, value in override.items():\n        if key in base and isinstance(base[key], dict) and isinstance(value, dict):\n            _deep_merge(base[key], value)\n        else:\n            base[key] = value\n    return base\n\n\ndef load_config(config_path: Path | None = None) -> AgentConfig:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the final AgentConfig by layering sources.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    config_path : Path, optional\n        Explicit path to a YAML file. If omitted the loader looks for\n        config.default.yaml then config.local.yaml inside configs/.\n    \"\"\"\n    merged: dict = {}\n\n    # Layer 1 \u2014 defaults\n    default_file = CONFIGS_DIR / \"config.default.yaml\"\n    if default_file.exists():\n        with open(default_file) as f:\n            _deep_merge(merged, yaml.safe_load(f) or {})\n\n    # Layer 2 \u2014 local overrides\n    local_file = CONFIGS_DIR / \"config.local.yaml\"\n    if local_file.exists():\n        with open(local_file) as f:\n            _deep_merge(merged, yaml.safe_load(f) or {})\n\n    # Layer 3 \u2014 explicit file (e.g. passed via CLI)\n    if config_path and config_path.exists():\n        with open(config_path) as f:\n            _deep_merge(merged, yaml.safe_load(f) or {})\n\n    # Layer 4 \u2014 environment variables (flat overrides for top-level keys)\n    env_mode = os.getenv(\"AGENT_MODE\")\n    if env_mode:\n        merged[\"mode\"] = env_mode\n\n    return AgentConfig(**merged)\n",

      "json_block": "{\"language\": \"python\", \"code\": \"\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nAbstract base class for all trading strategies.\\n\\nEvery strategy you write should subclass `BaseStrategy` and implement\\nthe `evaluate` method. The agent loop calls `evaluate()` on each tick\\nand acts on the returned Signal.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\\nfrom enum import Enum\\nfrom typing import Any\\n\\nimport pandas as pd\\n\\n\\nclass Signal(Enum):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"What the strategy wants the agent to do.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    BUY = \\\"BUY\\\"\\n    SELL = \\\"SELL\\\"\\n    HOLD = \\\"HOLD\\\"\\n\\n\\n@dataclass\\nclass TradeRecommendation:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"A strategy's output for one trading pair.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    pair: str\\n    signal: Signal\\n    confidence: float          # 0.0 \\u2013 1.0\\n    reason: str                # Human-readable explanation\\n    metadata: dict[str, Any]   # Extra info (indicator values, etc.)\\n\\n\\nclass BaseStrategy(ABC):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Interface that every strategy must implement.\\n\\n    Parameters\\n    ----------\\n    params : dict\\n        Strategy-specific parameters loaded from config.yaml\\n        (e.g. {\\\"fast_period\\\": 10, \\\"slow_period\\\": 30}).\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n        self.params = params\\n\\n    @property\\n    @abstractmethod\\n    def name(self) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Unique identifier for this strategy.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        ...\\n\\n    @property\\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Minimum number of candles needed before the strategy can evaluate.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        return 50  # sensible default; override in subclasses\\n\\n    @abstractmethod\\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Analyse recent candle data and return a trade recommendation.\\n\\n        Parameters\\n        ----------\\n        pair : str\\n            The trading pair, e.g. \\\"BTC/USDT\\\".\\n        candles : pd.DataFrame\\n            OHLCV data with columns: open, high, low, close, volume.\\n            Index is a DatetimeIndex. Most recent candle is the last row.\\n\\n        Returns\\n        -------\\n        TradeRecommendation\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        ...\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/crypto-trading-agent/src/agent/strategies/base.py\"}"
    },
    "approval_options": null,

      "file_text": "# Crypto Trading Agent\n\nAn automated cryptocurrency trading agent that evaluates markets, executes strategies, and manages a portfolio \u2014 starting safely with paper trading before ever touching real funds.\n\n> **\u26a0\ufe0f Disclaimer:** This is a personal learning and engineering project. Automated trading carries significant financial risk. Never trade with money you can't afford to lose. This software comes with no guarantees of profit. Consult a financial advisor before deploying real capital.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nThe agent is built as a pipeline of independent, testable components. Each component has a single responsibility and communicates through well-defined interfaces.\n\n```\n\u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n\u2502                   Agent Scheduler                    \u2502\n\u2502          (ticks every N seconds via APScheduler)     \u2502\n\u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n       \u2502              \u2502              \u2502\n       \u25bc              \u25bc              \u25bc\n \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510  \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510  \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n \u2502  Market   \u2502  \u2502 Strategy  \u2502  \u2502   Portfolio    \u2502\n \u2502   Data    \u2502  \u2502  Engine   \u2502  \u2502   Manager      \u2502\n \u2502   Feed    \u2502  \u2502           \u2502  \u2502                \u2502\n \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518  \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518  \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u252c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n       \u2502              \u2502               \u2502\n       \u2502         (evaluates)     (enforces risk\n       \u2502              \u2502          limits, tracks\n       \u25bc              \u25bc          positions)\n  \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510   \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510        \u2502\n  \u2502  ccxt   \u2502   \u2502 Indicators\u2502       \u25bc\n  \u2502 Exchange\u2502   \u2502 (ta lib)  \u2502  \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510\n  \u2502   API   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518  \u2502 Executor \u2502\n  \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518                 \u2502 (paper / \u2502\n                              \u2502  live)   \u2502\n                              \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518\n```\n\n### Component Responsibilities\n\n| Component | Location | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Config** | `src/agent/config.py` | Loads and validates all settings from YAML, env vars, and `.env` files using Pydantic. Single source of truth. |\n| **Market Data Feed** | `src/market_data/` | Connects to exchanges via `ccxt`, fetches OHLCV candles and order book data. Will support both REST polling and WebSocket streaming. |\n| **Strategy Engine** | `src/agent/strategies/` | Pluggable strategy system. Each strategy subclasses `BaseStrategy` and implements `evaluate()` \u2192 returns BUY / SELL / HOLD signals with confidence scores. |\n| **Indicators** | `src/agent/indicators/` | Technical analysis helpers (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, etc.) wrapping the `ta` library for easy use inside strategies. |\n| **Portfolio Manager** | `src/portfolio/` | Tracks all open positions, calculates P&L, enforces risk limits (max position size, stop-loss, daily loss cap). |\n| **Executor** | `src/execution/` | Translates trade signals into actual orders. `PaperExecutor` simulates fills locally; `LiveExecutor` submits real orders via ccxt. |\n| **Scheduler** | Part of `main.py` | Uses APScheduler to call the strategy at a fixed interval, feeding it fresh candle data each tick. |\n| **Logging** | `structlog` | Every decision, trade, and error is logged with structured context for easy debugging and audit trails. |\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\ncrypto-trading-agent/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 configs/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 config.default.yaml     # Default settings (committed)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 config.local.yaml       # Your overrides (git-ignored)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 data/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 historical/             # Downloaded candle data for backtesting\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 cache/                  # Temporary data cache\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 logs/                       # Agent log files\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 src/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 agent/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u2502   \u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 config.py           # Pydantic config system\n\u2502   \u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 main.py             # Entry point & component wiring\n\u2502   \u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 strategies/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 base.py         # BaseStrategy ABC & Signal types\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 indicators/\n\u2502   \u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 market_data/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 execution/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 portfolio/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 utils/\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 tests/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 __init__.py\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 test_config.py          # Config loading smoke tests\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 .env.example                # Template for API keys\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 .gitignore\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pyproject.toml              # Dependencies & project metadata\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 README.md                   # \u2190 You are here\n```\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# 1. Clone and enter the project\ncd crypto-trading-agent\n\n# 2. Create a virtual environment\npython3.11 -m venv .venv\nsource .venv/bin/activate\n\n# 3. Install in editable mode with dev dependencies\npip install -e \".[dev]\"\n\n# 4. Copy and configure your settings\ncp .env.example .env\ncp configs/config.default.yaml configs/config.local.yaml\n# Edit .env with your exchange API keys\n# Edit config.local.yaml with your preferences\n\n# 5. Run the tests\npytest\n\n# 6. Start the agent (paper mode by default)\ntrading-agent\n```\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nSettings are layered in this priority order (highest wins):\n\n1. `configs/config.default.yaml` \u2014 safe defaults shipped with the project\n2. `configs/config.local.yaml` \u2014 your personal overrides (git-ignored)\n3. Environment variables \u2014 prefixed `AGENT_` (e.g., `AGENT_MODE=live`)\n4. `.env` file \u2014 loaded automatically via python-dotenv\n\nAll config values are validated at startup by Pydantic. If something is wrong, the agent will fail fast with a clear error message rather than silently misbehaving.\n\n---\n\n## Roadmap\n\n### Phase 1 \u2014 Foundation \u2705 (current)\n- [x] Project scaffolding & folder structure\n- [x] Config system with validation\n- [x] Base strategy interface\n- [ ] Market data feed (ccxt REST polling)\n- [ ] Paper trading executor\n- [ ] Basic portfolio tracker\n- [ ] First strategy: SMA Crossover\n- [ ] Agent loop with scheduler\n\n### Phase 2 \u2014 Paper Trading & Backtesting\n- [ ] Backtesting engine (run strategies on historical data)\n- [ ] Performance metrics (Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, win rate)\n- [ ] Multiple strategy support\n- [ ] WebSocket streaming for real-time data\n\n### Phase 3 \u2014 Hardening & Monitoring\n- [ ] Comprehensive risk management (circuit breakers, kill switches)\n- [ ] Dashboard / monitoring UI\n- [ ] Alerting (Telegram, Discord, email notifications)\n- [ ] Thorough test coverage\n\n### Phase 4 \u2014 Live Trading (only after extensive paper testing)\n- [ ] Live executor with exchange order submission\n- [ ] Dry-run mode (submit & cancel immediately to verify flow)\n- [ ] Position reconciliation with exchange\n- [ ] Emergency stop button\n\n---\n\n## Safety Philosophy\n\n1. **Paper first, always.** The default mode is `paper`. You have to explicitly change it to go live.\n2. **Risk limits are mandatory.** Stop-losses, position caps, and daily loss limits are baked into the config and enforced by the portfolio manager.\n3. **Every decision is logged.** Full audit trail of every signal, trade, and state change.\n4. **Fail fast.** Invalid config, unreachable exchange, or unexpected data \u2192 the agent stops rather than guessing.\n",

Each component has a single responsibility and communicates through well-defined interfaces.\\n\\n```\\n\\u250c\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2510\\n\\u2502 \\u250c\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2510\\n \\u2502  Market   \\u2502  \\u2502 Strategy  \\u2502  \\u2502   Portfolio    \\u2502\\n \\u2502   Data    \\u2502  \\u2502  Engine   \\u2502  \\u2502   Manager      \\u2502\\n \\u2502   Feed    \\u2502  \\u2502           \\u2502  \\u2502                \\u2502\\n \\u250c\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2510   \\u250c\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2510        \\u2502\\n  \\u2502  ccxt   \\u2502   \\u2502 Indicators\\u2502       \\u25bc\\n  \\u2502 Exchange\\u2502   \\u2502 (ta lib)  \\u2502  \\u250c\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2510\\n  \\u2502   API   \\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2518  \\u2502 Executor \\u2502\\n        \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2518\\n```\\n\\n### Component Responsibilities\\n\\n| Component | Location | Purpose |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| **Config** | `src/agent/config.py` | Loads and validates all settings from YAML, env vars, and `.env` files using Pydantic. Single source of truth. |\\n| **Market Data Feed** | `src/market_data/` | Connects to exchanges via `ccxt`, fetches OHLCV candles and order book data. Will support both REST polling and WebSocket streaming. |\\n| **Strategy Engine** | `src/agent/strategies/` | Pluggable strategy system. Each strategy subclasses `BaseStrategy` and implements `evaluate()` \\u2192 returns BUY / SELL / HOLD signals with confidence scores. |\\n| **Indicators** | `src/agent/indicators/` | Technical analysis helpers (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, etc.) wrapping the `ta` library for easy use inside strategies. |\\n| **Portfolio Manager** | `src/portfolio/` | Tracks all open positions, calculates P&L, enforces risk limits (max position size, stop-loss, daily loss cap). |\\n| **Executor** | `src/execution/` | Translates trade signals into actual orders. `PaperExecutor` simulates fills locally; `LiveExecutor` submits real orders via ccxt. |\\n| **Scheduler** | Part of `main.py` | Uses APScheduler to call the strategy at a fixed interval, feeding it fresh candle data each tick. |\\n| **Logging** | `structlog` | Every decision, trade, and error is logged with structured context for easy debugging and audit trails. |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Project Structure\\n\\n```\\ncrypto-trading-agent/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 configs/\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 config.default.yaml     # Downloaded candle data for backtesting\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 cache/                  # Temporary data cache\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 logs/                       # Agent log files\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 src/\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 agent/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 __init__.py\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 config.py           # BaseStrategy ABC & Signal types\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 indicators/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502       \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 __init__.py\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 market_data/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 __init__.py\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 execution/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 __init__.py\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 portfolio/\\n\\u2502   \\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 __init__.py\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 utils/\\n\\u2502       \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 __init__.py\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 tests/\\n\\u2502   \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 __init__.py\\n\\u2502   \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 test_config.py          # \\u2190 You are here\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Quick Start\\n\\n```bash\\n# 1. Clone and enter the project\\ncd crypto-trading-agent\\n\\n# 2. Create a virtual environment\\npython3.11 -m venv .venv\\nsource .venv/bin/activate\\n\\n# 3. Environment variables \\u2014 prefixed `AGENT_` (e.g., `AGENT_MODE=live`)\\n4. `.env` file \\u2014 loaded automatically via python-dotenv\\n\\nAll config values are validated at startup by Pydantic.       "file_text": "\"\"\"\nMarket Data Feed \u2014 fetches OHLCV candle data from crypto exchanges.\n\nUses the `ccxt` library which provides a unified API across 100+ exchanges.\nSupports both one-time fetches and continuous polling.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\n\nimport ccxt\nimport pandas as pd\nimport structlog\n\nfrom agent.config import ExchangeConfig, TradingConfig\n\nlog = structlog.get_logger()\n\n\nclass MarketDataFeed:\n    \"\"\"\n    Connects to a crypto exchange and fetches candlestick (OHLCV) data.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    exchange_cfg : ExchangeConfig\n        Exchange name, sandbox mode, rate limits.\n    trading_cfg : TradingConfig\n        Which pairs and timeframe to fetch.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, exchange_cfg: ExchangeConfig, trading_cfg: TradingConfig) -> None:\n        self.exchange_cfg = exchange_cfg\n        self.trading_cfg = trading_cfg\n        self._exchange: ccxt.Exchange | None = None\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Connection\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def connect(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize the ccxt exchange connection.\"\"\"\n        exchange_class = getattr(ccxt, self.exchange_cfg.name, None)\n        if exchange_class is None:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Unknown exchange: '{self.exchange_cfg.name}'. \"\n                f\"Supported: {', '.join(ccxt.exchanges[:10])}...\"\n            )\n\n        self._exchange = exchange_class(\n            {\n                \"enableRateLimit\": True,\n                \"rateLimit\": self.exchange_cfg.rate_limit_ms,\n                \"sandbox\": self.exchange_cfg.sandbox,\n            }\n        )\n\n        # Some exchanges need markets loaded first\n        self._exchange.load_markets()\n\n        log.info(\n            \"market_data.connected\",\n            exchange=self.exchange_cfg.name,\n            sandbox=self.exchange_cfg.sandbox,\n            num_markets=len(self._exchange.markets),\n        )\n\n    @property\n    def exchange(self) -> ccxt.Exchange:\n        \"\"\"Return the live exchange connection, or raise if not connected.\"\"\"\n        if self._exchange is None:\n            raise RuntimeError(\"Call .connect() before using the market data feed.\")\n        return self._exchange\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Fetching candles\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def fetch_candles(\n        self,\n        pair: str,\n        timeframe: str | None = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch OHLCV candle data for a single trading pair.\n\n        Parameters\n        ----------\n        pair : str\n            e.g. \"BTC/USDT\"\n        timeframe : str, optional\n            e.g. \"1h\", \"15m\", \"1d\". Defaults to config value.\n        limit : int\n            Number of candles to fetch (max depends on exchange, usually 500\u20131000).\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        pd.DataFrame\n            Columns: open, high, low, close, volume\n            Index: DatetimeIndex (UTC)\n        \"\"\"\n        tf = timeframe or self.trading_cfg.default_timeframe\n\n        log.debug(\"market_data.fetching\", pair=pair, timeframe=tf, limit=limit)\n\n        raw = self.exchange.fetch_ohlcv(pair, timeframe=tf, limit=limit)\n\n        if not raw:\n            log.warning(\"market_data.empty\", pair=pair, timeframe=tf)\n            return pd.DataFrame(columns=[\"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"])\n\n        df = pd.DataFrame(raw, columns=[\"timestamp\", \"open\", \"high\", \"low\", \"close\", \"volume\"])\n        df[\"timestamp\"] = pd.to_datetime(df[\"timestamp\"], unit=\"ms\", utc=True)\n        df.set_index(\"timestamp\", inplace=True)\n\n        log.info(\n            \"market_data.fetched\",\n            pair=pair,\n            timeframe=tf,\n            candles=len(df),\n            latest=str(df.index[-1]),\n            close=float(df[\"close\"].iloc[-1]),\n        )\n\n        return df\n\n    def fetch_all_pairs(self, timeframe: str | None = None, limit: int = 100) -> dict[str, pd.DataFrame]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch candles for every pair listed in config.\n\n        Returns\n        -------\n        dict[str, pd.DataFrame]\n            Mapping of pair name \u2192 candle DataFrame.\n        \"\"\"\n        results: dict[str, pd.DataFrame] = {}\n\n        for pair in self.trading_cfg.pairs:\n            try:\n                results[pair] = self.fetch_candles(pair, timeframe=timeframe, limit=limit)\n            except Exception as e:\n                log.error(\"market_data.fetch_failed\", pair=pair, error=str(e))\n\n        return results\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Utility\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def get_current_price(self, pair: str) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Fetch the latest ticker price for a pair.\"\"\"\n        ticker = self.exchange.fetch_ticker(pair)\n        price = ticker.get(\"last\", 0.0)\n        log.debug(\"market_data.price\", pair=pair, price=price)\n        return float(price)\n\n    def get_all_prices(self) -> dict[str, float]:\n        \"\"\"Get current prices for all configured pairs.\"\"\"\n        prices: dict[str, float] = {}\n        for pair in self.trading_cfg.pairs:\n            try:\n                prices[pair] = self.get_current_price(pair)\n            except Exception as e:\n                log.error(\"market_data.price_failed\", pair=pair, error=str(e))\n        return prices\n",

     Order\n        \"\"\"\n        self._order_counter += 1\n        order_id = f\"PAPER-{self._order_counter:06d}\"\n\n        # Check if we have enough cash\n        if amount_usd > self.cash:\n -------\n        Order\n        \"\"\"\n        self._order_counter += 1\n        order_id = f\"PAPER-{self._order_counter:06d}\"\n\n        if pair not in self.positions:\n            order =       "description": "Create the portfolio manager \u2014 enforces risk limits and decides position sizing",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nPortfolio Manager \u2014 enforces risk limits and manages position sizing.\n\nActs as a safety layer between the strategy's signals and the executor.\nEven if a strategy says BUY, the portfolio manager can veto the trade\nif it would violate risk limits.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\n\nimport structlog\n\nfrom agent.config import RiskConfig\nfrom execution.paper import PaperExecutor, OrderSide, OrderStatus\n\nlog = structlog.get_logger()\n\n\nclass PortfolioManager:\n    \"\"\"\n    Wraps the executor and applies risk management rules before every trade.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    risk_cfg : RiskConfig\n        Max position sizes, stop-loss, daily loss limits.\n    executor : PaperExecutor\n        The executor that actually fills orders.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, risk_cfg: RiskConfig, executor: PaperExecutor) -> None:\n        self.risk = risk_cfg\n        self.executor = executor\n        self._daily_starting_value: float = executor.starting_balance\n        self._day_start: datetime = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n        self._halted: bool = False\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Risk checks\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def _check_daily_loss(self, current_prices: dict[str, float]) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if we've exceeded the max daily loss limit.\"\"\"\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n\n        # Reset daily tracker at midnight UTC\n        if now.date() > self._day_start.date():\n            self._daily_starting_value = self.executor.total_value(current_prices)\n            self._day_start = now\n            self._halted = False\n\n        current_value = self.executor.total_value(current_prices)\n        daily_pnl_pct = ((current_value - self._daily_starting_value) / self._daily_starting_value) * 100\n\n        if daily_pnl_pct <= -self.risk.max_daily_loss_pct:\n            log.error(\n                \"risk.daily_loss_limit_hit\",\n                daily_pnl_pct=round(daily_pnl_pct, 2),\n                limit=self.risk.max_daily_loss_pct,\n            )\n            self._halted = True\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    def _check_max_positions(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Return True if we've hit the max number of open positions.\"\"\"\n        return len(self.executor.positions) >= self.risk.max_open_positions\n\n    def _calculate_position_size(self, current_prices: dict[str, float]) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Calculate how much cash to use for a single trade based on risk limits.\"\"\"\n        total_value = self.executor.total_value(current_prices)\n        max_spend = total_value * (self.risk.max_position_pct / 100)\n        # Don't spend more than we have in cash\n        return min(max_spend, self.executor.cash)\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Trade execution with risk management\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def try_buy(\n        self,\n        pair: str,\n        price: float,\n        current_prices: dict[str, float],\n        reason: str = \"\",\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Attempt to open a position, subject to all risk checks.\n\n        Returns True if the order was filled, False if rejected.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Kill switch\n        if self._halted:\n            log.warning(\"risk.trading_halted\", pair=pair, msg=\"Daily loss limit exceeded\")\n            return False\n\n        if self._check_daily_loss(current_prices):\n            return False\n\n        # Already have a position in this pair?\n        if pair in self.executor.positions:\n            log.debug(\"risk.already_positioned\", pair=pair)\n            return False\n\n        # Max positions check\n        if self._check_max_positions():\n            log.warning(\n                \"risk.max_positions_reached\",\n                pair=pair,\n                limit=self.risk.max_open_positions,\n            )\n            return False\n\n        # Calculate safe position size\n        amount = self._calculate_position_size(current_prices)\n        if amount < 1.0:  # minimum trade size\n            log.warning(\"risk.insufficient_funds\", pair=pair, available=round(amount, 2))\n            return False\n\n        order = self.executor.buy(pair, amount, price, reason)\n        return order.status == OrderStatus.FILLED\n\n    def try_sell(\n        self,\n        pair: str,\n        price: float,\n        reason: str = \"\",\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Attempt to close a position.\n\n        Returns True if the order was filled, False if rejected.\n        \"\"\"\n        order = self.executor.sell(pair, price, reason)\n        return order.status == OrderStatus.FILLED\n\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    # Stop-loss / take-profit checks\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n    def check_stop_loss_take_profit(self, current_prices: dict[str, float]) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check all open positions for stop-loss or take-profit triggers.\n\n        Returns a list of pairs that were closed.\n        \"\"\"\n        closed: list[str] = []\n\n        # Iterate over a copy since we might modify positions during iteration\n        for pair, pos in list(self.executor.positions.items()):\n            current_price = current_prices.get(pair)\n            if current_price is None:\n                continue\n\n            pnl_pct = pos.unrealized_pnl_pct(current_price)\n\n            # Stop-loss\n            if pnl_pct <= -self.risk.stop_loss_pct:\n                log.warning(\n                    \"risk.stop_loss_triggered\",\n                    pair=pair,\n                    pnl_pct=round(pnl_pct, 2),\n                    limit=self.risk.stop_loss_pct,\n                )\n                self.try_sell(pair, current_price, reason=f\"Stop-loss at {pnl_pct:.1f}%\")\n                closed.append(pair)\n\n            # Take-profit\n            elif pnl_pct >= self.risk.take_profit_pct:\n                log.info(\n                    \"risk.take_profit_triggered\",\n                    pair=pair,\n                    pnl_pct=round(pnl_pct, 2),\n                    limit=self.risk.take_profit_pct,\n                )\n                self.try_sell(pair, current_price, reason=f\"Take-profit at {pnl_pct:.1f}%\")\n                closed.append(pair)\n\n        return closed\n",

------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    # Risk checks\\n    # ------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    def _check_daily_loss(self, current_prices: dict[str, float]) -> bool:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return True if we've exceeded the max daily loss limit.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\\n\\n        # Reset daily tracker at midnight UTC\\n        if now.date() > self._day_start.date():\\n            self._daily_starting_value = self.executor.total_value(current_prices)\\n            self._day_start = now\\n            self._halted = False\\n\\n        current_value = self.executor.total_value(current_prices)\\n        daily_pnl_pct = ((current_value - self._daily_starting_value) / self._daily_starting_value) * 100\\n\\n        if daily_pnl_pct <= -self.risk.max_daily_loss_pct:\\n            log.error(\\n                \\\"risk.daily_loss_limit_hit\\\",\\n                daily_pnl_pct=round(daily_pnl_pct, 2),\\n                limit=self.risk.max_daily_loss_pct,\\n            )\\n            self._halted = True\\n            return True\\n        return False\\n\\n    def _check_max_positions(self) -> bool:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Return True if we've hit the max number of open positions.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        return len(self.executor.positions) >= self.risk.max_open_positions\\n\\n    def _calculate_position_size(self, current_prices: dict[str, float]) -> float:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Calculate how much cash to use for a single trade based on risk limits.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        total_value = self.executor.total_value(current_prices)\\n        max_spend = total_value * (self.risk.max_position_pct / 100)\\n        # Already have a position in this pair?\\n        if pair in self.executor.positions:\\n            log.debug(\\\"risk.already_positioned\\\", pair=pair)\\n            return False\\n\\n        # Max positions check\\n        if self._check_max_positions():\\n            log.warning(\\n                \\\"risk.max_positions_reached\\\",\\n                pair=pair,\\n                limit=self.risk.max_open_positions,\\n            )\\n            return False\\n\\n        #       "file_text": "\"\"\"\nSMA Crossover Strategy \u2014 the classic moving-average crossover.\n\nGenerates a BUY signal when the fast SMA crosses above the slow SMA,\nand a SELL signal when the fast SMA crosses below the slow SMA.\n\nThis is a simple trend-following strategy. It won't make you rich, but\nit's a solid starting point to validate that the whole pipeline works.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\n\n\nclass SMACrossoverStrategy(BaseStrategy):\n    \"\"\"\n    Simple Moving Average crossover strategy.\n\n    Config params:\n        fast_period (int): Number of candles for the fast SMA. Default 10.\n        slow_period (int): Number of candles for the slow SMA. Default 30.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @property\n    def name(self) -> str:\n        return \"sma_crossover\"\n\n    @property\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\n        return self.params.get(\"slow_period\", 30) + 5  # a few extra for safety\n\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\n        fast_period = self.params.get(\"fast_period\", 10)\n        slow_period = self.params.get(\"slow_period\", 30)\n\n        if len(candles) < slow_period + 2:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\n                confidence=0.0,\n                reason=f\"Not enough data: have {len(candles)} candles, need {slow_period + 2}\",\n                metadata={},\n            )\n\n        # Calculate SMAs\n        candles = candles.copy()\n        candles[\"sma_fast\"] = candles[\"close\"].rolling(window=fast_period).mean()\n        candles[\"sma_slow\"] = candles[\"close\"].rolling(window=slow_period).mean()\n\n        # Get the last two data points to detect a crossover\n        current = candles.iloc[-1]\n        previous = candles.iloc[-2]\n\n        fast_now = current[\"sma_fast\"]\n        slow_now = current[\"sma_slow\"]\n        fast_prev = previous[\"sma_fast\"]\n        slow_prev = previous[\"sma_slow\"]\n        price_now = current[\"close\"]\n\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"sma_fast\": round(fast_now, 2),\n            \"sma_slow\": round(slow_now, 2),\n            \"price\": round(price_now, 2),\n            \"fast_period\": fast_period,\n            \"slow_period\": slow_period,\n        }\n\n        # Bullish crossover: fast crosses above slow\n        if fast_prev <= slow_prev and fast_now > slow_now:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=0.7,\n                reason=f\"SMA bullish crossover: fast({fast_period})={fast_now:.2f} crossed above slow({slow_period})={slow_now:.2f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # Bearish crossover: fast crosses below slow\n        if fast_prev >= slow_prev and fast_now < slow_now:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=0.7,\n                reason=f\"SMA bearish crossover: fast({fast_period})={fast_now:.2f} crossed below slow({slow_period})={slow_now:.2f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # No crossover \u2014 hold\n        gap_pct = ((fast_now - slow_now) / slow_now) * 100 if slow_now else 0\n        trend = \"bullish\" if fast_now > slow_now else \"bearish\"\n\n        return TradeRecommendation(\n            pair=pair,\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\n            confidence=0.5,\n            reason=f\"No crossover. Trend: {trend}, SMA gap: {gap_pct:.2f}%\",\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n",

      "description": "Create the agent loop \u2014 the main orchestrator that runs the strategy on a schedule",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nAgent Loop \u2014 the main orchestrator.\n\nOn each tick:\n  1. Fetch fresh candle data\n  2. Check stop-loss / take-profit on open positions\n  3. Run the strategy on each pair\n  4. Execute any BUY/SELL signals through the portfolio manager\n  5. Log the portfolio summary\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport time\n\nimport structlog\n\nfrom agent.config import AgentConfig\nfrom agent.strategies.base import Signal, BaseStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.loader import load_strategy\nfrom market_data.feed import MarketDataFeed\nfrom execution.paper import PaperExecutor\nfrom portfolio.manager import PortfolioManager\n\nlog = structlog.get_logger()\n\n\nclass AgentLoop:\n    \"\"\"\n    Wires all components together and runs the trading loop.\n\n    Parameters\n    ----------\n    cfg : AgentConfig\n        The full, validated agent configuration.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, cfg: AgentConfig) -> None:\n        self.cfg = cfg\n        self.tick_count: int = 0\n\n        # Build components\n        self.feed = MarketDataFeed(cfg.exchange, cfg.trading)\n        self.strategy: BaseStrategy = load_strategy(cfg.strategy.name, cfg.strategy.params)\n        self.executor = PaperExecutor(cfg.paper.starting_balance)\n        self.portfolio = PortfolioManager(cfg.risk, self.executor)\n\n    def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Connect to the exchange and begin the loop.\"\"\"\n        log.info(\n            \"agent_loop.starting\",\n            strategy=self.strategy.name,\n            pairs=self.cfg.trading.pairs,\n            interval_s=self.cfg.scheduler.strategy_interval_seconds,\n            mode=self.cfg.mode,\n        )\n\n        self.feed.connect()\n\n        log.info(\"agent_loop.running\", msg=\"Agent is live. Press Ctrl+C to stop.\")\n\n        try:\n            while True:\n                self._tick()\n                time.sleep(self.cfg.scheduler.strategy_interval_seconds)\n        except KeyboardInterrupt:\n            log.info(\"agent_loop.stopped\", msg=\"Shutdown by user (Ctrl+C)\")\n            self._print_final_summary()\n\n    def _tick(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Execute one cycle of the trading loop.\"\"\"\n        self.tick_count += 1\n        log.info(\"agent_loop.tick\", tick=self.tick_count)\n\n        # Step 1: Fetch current prices for risk checks\n        current_prices = self.feed.get_all_prices()\n        if not current_prices:\n            log.warning(\"agent_loop.no_prices\", msg=\"Could not fetch prices, skipping tick\")\n            return\n\n        # Step 2: Check stop-loss / take-profit on open positions\n        closed = self.portfolio.check_stop_loss_take_profit(current_prices)\n        if closed:\n            log.info(\"agent_loop.auto_closed\", pairs=closed)\n\n        # Step 3: Fetch candles and run strategy on each pair\n        all_candles = self.feed.fetch_all_pairs(limit=self.strategy.required_history + 10)\n\n        for pair, candles in all_candles.items():\n            if candles.empty:\n                continue\n\n            # Run the strategy\n            recommendation = self.strategy.evaluate(pair, candles)\n\n            log.info(\n                \"agent_loop.signal\",\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=recommendation.signal.value,\n                confidence=recommendation.confidence,\n                reason=recommendation.reason,\n            )\n\n            # Step 4: Execute signals through the portfolio manager\n            if recommendation.signal == Signal.BUY:\n                price = current_prices.get(pair, 0)\n                if price > 0:\n                    self.portfolio.try_buy(\n                        pair=pair,\n                        price=price,\n                        current_prices=current_prices,\n                        reason=recommendation.reason,\n                    )\n\n            elif recommendation.signal == Signal.SELL:\n                price = current_prices.get(pair, 0)\n                if price > 0:\n                    self.portfolio.try_sell(\n                        pair=pair,\n                        price=price,\n                        reason=recommendation.reason,\n                    )\n\n        # Step 5: Log portfolio summary\n        summary = self.executor.summary(current_prices)\n        log.info(\n            \"agent_loop.portfolio\",\n            cash=summary[\"cash\"],\n            total_value=summary[\"total_value\"],\n            pnl=summary[\"total_pnl\"],\n            pnl_pct=summary[\"total_pnl_pct\"],\n            open_positions=summary[\"open_positions\"],\n            total_trades=summary[\"total_trades\"],\n        )\n\n    def _print_final_summary(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Print a final report when the agent shuts down.\"\"\"\n        current_prices = self.feed.get_all_prices()\n        summary = self.executor.summary(current_prices)\n\n        log.info(\"=\" * 60)\n        log.info(\"agent_loop.final_report\")\n        log.info(f\"  Total ticks:      {self.tick_count}\")\n        log.info(f\"  Total trades:     {summary['total_trades']}\")\n        log.info(f\"  Starting balance: ${self.executor.starting_balance:,.2f}\")\n        log.info(f\"  Final value:      ${summary['total_value']:,.2f}\")\n        log.info(f\"  P&L:              ${summary['total_pnl']:,.2f} ({summary['total_pnl_pct']:.2f}%)\")\n        log.info(f\"  Cash remaining:   ${summary['cash']:,.2f}\")\n        log.info(f\"  Open positions:   {summary['open_positions']}\")\n        log.info(\"=\" * 60)\n",

0.01 BTC\n\n\ndef test_buy_rejected_insufficient_funds():\n    \"\"\"Buying more than available cash should be rejected.\"\"\"\n    ex = PaperExecutor(starting_balance=100)\n    order = ex.buy(\"BTC/USDT\", amount_usd=500, price=50_000, reason=\"test\")\n\n    assert order.status == OrderStatus.REJECTED\n    assert \"BTC/USDT\" not in ex.positions\n    assert ex.cash == 100  #       "file_text": "\"\"\"Tests for the SMA Crossover strategy.\"\"\"\n\nimport numpy as np\nimport pandas as pd\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import Signal\nfrom agent.strategies.sma_crossover import SMACrossoverStrategy\n\n\ndef _make_candles(prices: list[float]) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"Helper: build a candle DataFrame from a list of close prices.\"\"\"\n    n = len(prices)\n    df = pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"open\": prices,\n            \"high\": [p * 1.01 for p in prices],\n            \"low\": [p * 0.99 for p in prices],\n            \"close\": prices,\n            \"volume\": [1000.0] * n,\n        },\n        index=pd.date_range(\"2025-01-01\", periods=n, freq=\"h\", tz=\"UTC\"),\n    )\n    return df\n\n\ndef test_bullish_crossover_generates_buy():\n    \"\"\"When fast SMA crosses above slow SMA \u2192 BUY signal.\"\"\"\n    strategy = SMACrossoverStrategy(params={\"fast_period\": 3, \"slow_period\": 5})\n\n    # Build prices where the last few candles create a bullish crossover\n    # Slow decline \u2192 fast recovery\n    prices = [100, 99, 98, 97, 96, 95, 94, 93, 92, 91,   # downtrend\n              92, 94, 97, 100, 105]                          # fast recovery\n\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    # The fast SMA should have crossed above the slow SMA\n    assert result.signal == Signal.BUY\n    assert result.confidence > 0\n    assert \"bullish\" in result.reason.lower()\n\n\ndef test_bearish_crossover_generates_sell():\n    \"\"\"When fast SMA crosses below slow SMA \u2192 SELL signal.\"\"\"\n    strategy = SMACrossoverStrategy(params={\"fast_period\": 3, \"slow_period\": 5})\n\n    # Build prices: uptrend \u2192 sharp decline\n    prices = [90, 91, 92, 93, 94, 95, 96, 97, 98, 100,  # uptrend\n              98, 95, 90, 85, 80]                           # decline\n\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    assert result.signal == Signal.SELL\n    assert result.confidence > 0\n    assert \"bearish\" in result.reason.lower()\n\n\ndef test_hold_when_no_crossover():\n    \"\"\"Flat prices \u2192 HOLD signal.\"\"\"\n    strategy = SMACrossoverStrategy(params={\"fast_period\": 3, \"slow_period\": 5})\n\n    # Very flat prices \u2014 no crossover\n    prices = [100.0] * 20\n\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    assert result.signal == Signal.HOLD\n\n\ndef test_hold_when_insufficient_data():\n    \"\"\"Not enough candles \u2192 HOLD with explanation.\"\"\"\n    strategy = SMACrossoverStrategy(params={\"fast_period\": 3, \"slow_period\": 10})\n\n    prices = [100.0] * 5  # only 5 candles, need 12+\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    assert result.signal == Signal.HOLD\n    assert \"not enough\" in result.reason.lower()\n\n\ndef test_strategy_loader():\n    \"\"\"The strategy loader should find sma_crossover by name.\"\"\"\n    from agent.strategies.loader import load_strategy\n\n    strategy = load_strategy(\"sma_crossover\", {\"fast_period\": 5, \"slow_period\": 20})\n    assert strategy.name == \"sma_crossover\"\n    assert strategy.params[\"fast_period\"] == 5\n",

Get the last two data points to detect a crossover\n    56\t        current = candles.iloc[-1]\n    57\t        previous = candles.iloc[-2]\n    58\t\n    59\t        fast_now = current[\"sma_fast\"]\n    60\t        slow_now = current[\"sma_slow\"]\n    61\t        fast_prev = previous[\"sma_fast\"]\n    62\t        slow_prev = previous[\"sma_slow\"]\n    63\t        price_now = current[\"close\"]\n    64\t\n    65\t        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\n    66\t            \"sma_fast\": round(fast_now, 2),\n    67\t            \"sma_slow\": round(slow_now, 2),\n    68\t            \"price\": round(price_now, 2),\n    69\t            \"fast_period\": fast_period,\n    70\t            \"slow_period\": slow_period,\n    71\t        }\n    72\t\n    73\t        # Bullish crossover: fast crosses above slow\n    74\t        if fast_prev <= slow_prev and fast_now > slow_now:\n    75\t            return TradeRecommendation(\n    76\t                pair=pair,\n    77\t                signal=Signal.BUY,\n    78\t                confidence=0.7,\n    79\t                reason=f\"SMA bullish crossover: fast({fast_period})={fast_now:.2f} crossed above slow({slow_period})={slow_now:.2f}\",\n    80\t                metadata=metadata,\n    81\t            )\n    82\t\n    83\t        #       "file_text": "\"\"\"\nRSI (Relative Strength Index) Strategy.\n\nBuys when RSI drops below the oversold threshold (asset is cheap),\nsells when RSI rises above the overbought threshold (asset is expensive).\n\nRSI ranges from 0 to 100:\n  - Below 30 = oversold (potential buy)\n  - Above 70 = overbought (potential sell)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\n\n\nclass RSIStrategy(BaseStrategy):\n    \"\"\"\n    RSI-based mean-reversion strategy.\n\n    Config params:\n        period (int): RSI lookback period. Default 14.\n        oversold (float): Buy threshold. Default 30.\n        overbought (float): Sell threshold. Default 70.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @property\n    def name(self) -> str:\n        return \"rsi\"\n\n    @property\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\n        return self.params.get(\"period\", 14) + 10\n\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\n        period = self.params.get(\"period\", 14)\n        oversold = self.params.get(\"oversold\", 30)\n        overbought = self.params.get(\"overbought\", 70)\n\n        if len(candles) < period + 2:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\n                confidence=0.0,\n                reason=f\"Not enough data: have {len(candles)}, need {period + 2}\",\n                metadata={},\n            )\n\n        # Calculate RSI\n        delta = candles[\"close\"].diff()\n        gain = delta.where(delta > 0, 0.0).rolling(window=period).mean()\n        loss = (-delta.where(delta < 0, 0.0)).rolling(window=period).mean()\n\n        rs = gain / loss.replace(0, float(\"nan\"))\n        rsi = 100 - (100 / (1 + rs))\n\n        current_rsi = float(rsi.iloc[-1])\n        prev_rsi = float(rsi.iloc[-2])\n        price = float(candles[\"close\"].iloc[-1])\n\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"rsi\": round(current_rsi, 2),\n            \"prev_rsi\": round(prev_rsi, 2),\n            \"price\": round(price, 2),\n            \"period\": period,\n            \"oversold\": oversold,\n            \"overbought\": overbought,\n        }\n\n        # RSI crossing below oversold \u2192 BUY\n        if current_rsi < oversold and prev_rsi >= oversold:\n            confidence = min(1.0, (oversold - current_rsi) / oversold)\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=round(confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"RSI crossed below oversold: RSI={current_rsi:.1f} < {oversold}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # Already oversold \u2192 BUY (weaker signal)\n        if current_rsi < oversold:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=0.5,\n                reason=f\"RSI in oversold zone: RSI={current_rsi:.1f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # RSI crossing above overbought \u2192 SELL\n        if current_rsi > overbought and prev_rsi <= overbought:\n            confidence = min(1.0, (current_rsi - overbought) / (100 - overbought))\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=round(confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"RSI crossed above overbought: RSI={current_rsi:.1f} > {overbought}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # Already overbought \u2192 SELL (weaker signal)\n        if current_rsi > overbought:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=0.5,\n                reason=f\"RSI in overbought zone: RSI={current_rsi:.1f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        return TradeRecommendation(\n            pair=pair,\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\n            confidence=0.3,\n            reason=f\"RSI neutral: {current_rsi:.1f} (range: {oversold}-{overbought})\",\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n",

      "description": "Create MACD strategy",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nMACD (Moving Average Convergence Divergence) Strategy.\n\nMACD measures the relationship between two exponential moving averages.\nWhen the MACD line crosses above the signal line \u2192 bullish (BUY).\nWhen it crosses below \u2192 bearish (SELL).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\n\n\nclass MACDStrategy(BaseStrategy):\n    \"\"\"\n    MACD crossover strategy.\n\n    Config params:\n        fast_period (int): Fast EMA period. Default 12.\n        slow_period (int): Slow EMA period. Default 26.\n        signal_period (int): Signal line EMA period. Default 9.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @property\n    def name(self) -> str:\n        return \"macd\"\n\n    @property\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\n        return self.params.get(\"slow_period\", 26) + self.params.get(\"signal_period\", 9) + 5\n\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\n        fast = self.params.get(\"fast_period\", 12)\n        slow = self.params.get(\"slow_period\", 26)\n        signal_period = self.params.get(\"signal_period\", 9)\n\n        min_needed = slow + signal_period + 2\n        if len(candles) < min_needed:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\n                confidence=0.0,\n                reason=f\"Not enough data: have {len(candles)}, need {min_needed}\",\n                metadata={},\n            )\n\n        close = candles[\"close\"]\n\n        # Calculate MACD components\n        ema_fast = close.ewm(span=fast, adjust=False).mean()\n        ema_slow = close.ewm(span=slow, adjust=False).mean()\n        macd_line = ema_fast - ema_slow\n        signal_line = macd_line.ewm(span=signal_period, adjust=False).mean()\n        histogram = macd_line - signal_line\n\n        macd_now = float(macd_line.iloc[-1])\n        macd_prev = float(macd_line.iloc[-2])\n        signal_now = float(signal_line.iloc[-1])\n        signal_prev = float(signal_line.iloc[-2])\n        hist_now = float(histogram.iloc[-1])\n        price = float(close.iloc[-1])\n\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"macd\": round(macd_now, 4),\n            \"signal\": round(signal_now, 4),\n            \"histogram\": round(hist_now, 4),\n            \"price\": round(price, 2),\n        }\n\n        # Bullish crossover: MACD crosses above signal\n        if macd_prev <= signal_prev and macd_now > signal_now:\n            strength = abs(hist_now) / price * 1000  # normalized\n            confidence = min(0.9, 0.5 + strength)\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=round(confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"MACD bullish crossover: MACD={macd_now:.4f} crossed above signal={signal_now:.4f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # Bearish crossover: MACD crosses below signal\n        if macd_prev >= signal_prev and macd_now < signal_now:\n            strength = abs(hist_now) / price * 1000\n            confidence = min(0.9, 0.5 + strength)\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=round(confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"MACD bearish crossover: MACD={macd_now:.4f} crossed below signal={signal_now:.4f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        trend = \"bullish\" if macd_now > signal_now else \"bearish\"\n        return TradeRecommendation(\n            pair=pair,\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\n            confidence=0.3,\n            reason=f\"No MACD crossover. Trend: {trend}, histogram: {hist_now:.4f}\",\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n",

    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_019tGMUEFDrmsM5kMh1SsC87",
    "name": "create_file",
    "input": {
      "description": "Create Bollinger Bands strategy",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nBollinger Bands Strategy.\n\nBollinger Bands create a channel around the price using standard deviations.\nWhen price touches the lower band \u2192 potential buy (oversold).\nWhen price touches the upper band \u2192 potential sell (overbought).\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\n\n\nclass BollingerBandsStrategy(BaseStrategy):\n    \"\"\"\n    Bollinger Bands mean-reversion strategy.\n\n    Config params:\n        period (int): Moving average period. Default 20.\n        std_dev (float): Number of standard deviations. Default 2.0.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @property\n    def name(self) -> str:\n        return \"bollinger_bands\"\n\n    @property\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\n        return self.params.get(\"period\", 20) + 5\n\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\n        period = self.params.get(\"period\", 20)\n        std_dev = self.params.get(\"std_dev\", 2.0)\n\n        if len(candles) < period + 2:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\n                confidence=0.0,\n                reason=f\"Not enough data: have {len(candles)}, need {period + 2}\",\n                metadata={},\n            )\n\n        close = candles[\"close\"]\n        sma = close.rolling(window=period).mean()\n        std = close.rolling(window=period).std()\n\n        upper_band = sma + (std * std_dev)\n        lower_band = sma - (std * std_dev)\n\n        price = float(close.iloc[-1])\n        prev_price = float(close.iloc[-2])\n        upper = float(upper_band.iloc[-1])\n        lower = float(lower_band.iloc[-1])\n        middle = float(sma.iloc[-1])\n\n        # %B indicator: where price sits within the bands (0 = lower, 1 = upper)\n        band_width = upper - lower\n        pct_b = (price - lower) / band_width if band_width > 0 else 0.5\n\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"price\": round(price, 2),\n            \"upper_band\": round(upper, 2),\n            \"lower_band\": round(lower, 2),\n            \"middle_band\": round(middle, 2),\n            \"pct_b\": round(pct_b, 4),\n            \"band_width\": round(band_width, 2),\n        }\n\n        # Price crosses below lower band \u2192 BUY (oversold bounce)\n        if price <= lower and prev_price > lower:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=0.75,\n                reason=f\"Price broke below lower Bollinger Band: ${price:.2f} < ${lower:.2f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # Price well below lower band \u2192 strong BUY\n        if price < lower:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=0.6,\n                reason=f\"Price below lower band: ${price:.2f}, %B={pct_b:.2f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # Price crosses above upper band \u2192 SELL (overbought)\n        if price >= upper and prev_price < upper:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=0.75,\n                reason=f\"Price broke above upper Bollinger Band: ${price:.2f} > ${upper:.2f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # Price well above upper band \u2192 strong SELL\n        if price > upper:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=0.6,\n                reason=f\"Price above upper band: ${price:.2f}, %B={pct_b:.2f}\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        return TradeRecommendation(\n            pair=pair,\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\n            confidence=0.3,\n            reason=f\"Price within bands: %B={pct_b:.2f} (0=lower, 1=upper)\",\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n",

Default 20.\\n        std_dev (float): Number of standard deviations. Default 2.0.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def name(self) -> str:\\n        return \\\"bollinger_bands\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\\n        return self.params.get(\\\"period\\\", 20) + 5\\n\\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\\n        period = self.params.get(\\\"period\\\", 20)\\n        std_dev = self.params.get(\\\"std_dev\\\", 2.0)\\n\\n        if len(candles) < period + 2:\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\\n                confidence=0.0,\\n                reason=f\\\"Not enough data: have {len(candles)}, need {period + 2}\\\",\\n                metadata={},\\n            )\\n\\n        close = candles[\\\"close\\\"]\\n        sma = close.rolling(window=period).mean()\\n        std = close.rolling(window=period).std()\\n\\n        upper_band = sma + (std * std_dev)\\n        lower_band = sma - (std * std_dev)\\n\\n        price = float(close.iloc[-1])\\n        prev_price = float(close.iloc[-2])\\n        upper = float(upper_band.iloc[-1])\\n        lower = float(lower_band.iloc[-1])\\n        middle = float(sma.iloc[-1])\\n\\n        # Price well above upper band \\u2192 strong SELL\\n        if price > upper:\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\\n                confidence=0.6,\\n                reason=f\\\"Price above upper band: ${price:.2f}, %B={pct_b:.2f}\\\",\\n                metadata=metadata,\\n            )\\n\\n        return TradeRecommendation(\\n            pair=pair,\\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\\n            confidence=0.3,\\n            reason=f\\\"Price within bands: %B={pct_b:.2f} (0=lower, 1=upper)\\\",\\n            metadata=metadata,\\n        )\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/crypto-trading-agent/src/agent/strategies/bollinger_bands.py\"}"
