${s.strategy || '--'} | ${s.timeframe || '--'} | Tick #${s.tick || 0}`;\n\n  // Price ticker\n  updateTicker(s.prices || {});\n\n  // Portfolio cards\n  updatePortfolioCards(s.portfolio || {});\n\n  // Positions\n  updatePositions(s.portfolio?.positions || {});\n\n  // Signals (advanced)\n  updateSignals(s.signals || []);\n\n  // Trade log\n  updateTrades(s.trade_log || []);\n\n  // Equity chart (advanced)\n  updateEquityChart(s.equity_curve || []);\n}\n\nfunction updateTicker(prices) {\n  const el = document.getElementById('ticker');\n  if (!Object.keys(prices).length) { el.innerHTML = '<div class=\"ticker-item\"><span class=\"meta-text\">Waiting for price data...</span></div>'; return; }\n  el.innerHTML = Object.entries(prices).map(([pair, price]) =>\n    `<div class=\"ticker-item\">\n      <span class=\"ticker-pair\">${pair}</span>\n      <span class=\"ticker-price\">$${Number(price).toLocaleString(undefined, {minimumFractionDigits:2, maximumFractionDigits:2})}</span>\n    </div>`\n  ).join('');\n}\n\nfunction updatePortfolioCards(p) {\n  const pnlClass = (p.total_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative';\n  const pnlSign = (p.total_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? '+' : '';\n\n  let html = `\n    <div class=\"card\">\n      <div class=\"card-label\">Portfolio Value</div>\n      <div class=\"card-value neutral\">$${(p.total_value || 0).toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2})}</div>\n    </div>\n    <div class=\"card\">\n      <div class=\"card-label\">P&L</div>\n      <div class=\"card-value ${pnlClass}\">${pnlSign}$${(p.total_pnl || 0).toFixed(2)}</div>\n      <div class=\"card-sub\">${pnlSign}${(p.total_pnl_pct || 0).toFixed(2)}%</div>\n    </div>\n    <div class=\"card\">\n      <div class=\"card-label\">Cash</div>\n      <div class=\"card-value neutral\">$${(p.cash || 0).toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2})}</div>\n    </div>\n    <div class=\"card\">\n      <div class=\"card-label\">Open Positions</div>\n      <div class=\"card-value neutral\">${p.open_positions || 0}</div>\n    </div>`;\n\n  // Advanced-only cards\n  html += `\n    <div class=\"card advanced-only\">\n      <div class=\"card-label\">Total Trades</div>\n      <div class=\"card-value neutral\">${p.total_trades || 0}</div>\n    </div>`;\n\n  document.getElementById('portfolioCards').innerHTML = html;\n}\n\nfunction updatePositions(positions) {\n  const section = document.getElementById('positionsSection');\n  const body = document.getElementById('positionsBody');\n  const entries = Object.entries(positions);\n\n  if (!entries.length) { section.style.display = 'none'; return; }\n  section.style.display = 'block';\n\n  body.innerHTML = entries.map(([pair, pos]) => {\n    const pnlClass = (pos.unrealized_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative';\n    const sign = (pos.unrealized_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? '+' : '';\n    return `<tr>\n      <td><strong>${pair}</strong></td>\n      <td>${pos.quantity}</td>\n      <td>$${(pos.entry_price||0).toLocaleString()}</td>\n      <td>$${(pos.current_price||0).toLocaleString()}</td>\n      <td class=\"${pnlClass}\">${sign}$${(pos.unrealized_pnl||0).toFixed(2)} (${sign}${(pos.unrealized_pnl_pct||0).toFixed(2)}%)</td>\n    </tr>`;\n  }).join('');\n}\n\nfunction updateSignals(signals) {\n  const body = document.getElementById('signalsBody');\n  const recent = signals.slice(-15).reverse();\n  body.innerHTML = recent.map(s => {\n    const badgeClass = s.signal === 'BUY' ? 'badge-buy' : s.signal === 'SELL' ? 'badge-sell' : 'badge-hold';\n    return `<tr>\n      <td>${(s.time||'').slice(11,19)}</td>\n      <td>${s.pair}</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge ${badgeClass}\">${s.signal}</span></td>\n      <td>${(s.confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%</td>\n      <td style=\"font-size:11px;color:#8b949e;max-width:300px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap\">${s.reason}</td>\n    </tr>`;\n  }).join('');\n}\n\nfunction updateTrades(trades) {\n  const body = document.getElementById('tradesBody');\n  if (!trades.length) {\n    body.innerHTML = '<tr><td colspan=\"6\" style=\"color:#8b949e;text-align:center\">No trades yet \u2014 waiting for signals...</td></tr>';\n    return;\n  }\n  const recent = trades.slice(-20).reverse();\n  body.innerHTML = recent.map(t => {\n    const badgeClass = t.side === 'BUY' ? 'badge-buy' : 'badge-sell';\n    return `<tr>\n      <td>${(t.time||'').slice(11,19)}</td>\n      <td>${t.pair}</td>\n      <td><span class=\"badge ${badgeClass}\">${t.side}</span></td>\n      <td>$${(t.price||0).toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2})}</td>\n      <td>$${(t.cost||0).toFixed(2)}</td>\n      <td style=\"font-size:11px;color:#8b949e;max-width:250px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap\">${t.reason||''}</td>\n    </tr>`;\n  }).join('');\n}\n\nfunction updateEquityChart(curve) {\n  const canvas = document.getElementById('equityChart');\n  if (!curve.length) return;\n\n  const labels = curve.map(p => p.time ? p.time.slice(11, 19) : '');\n  const data = curve.map(p => p.value);\n  const startVal = data[0] || 10000;\n  const isPositive = data[data.length - 1] >= startVal;\n\n  if (equityChart) {\n    equityChart.data.labels = labels;\n    equityChart.data.datasets[0].data = data;\n    equityChart.data.datasets[0].borderColor = isPositive ? '#3fb950' : '#f85149';\n    equityChart.data.datasets[0].backgroundColor = isPositive ? 'rgba(63,185,80,0.08)' : 'rgba(248,81,73,0.08)';\n    equityChart.data.datasets[1].data = labels.map(() => startVal);\n    equityChart.update('none');\n    return;\n  }\n\n  equityChart = new Chart(canvas, {\n    type: 'line',\n    data: {\n      labels,\n      datasets: [\n        {\n          label: 'Portfolio ($)',\n          data,\n          borderColor: isPositive ? '#3fb950' : '#f85149',\n          backgroundColor: isPositive ? 'rgba(63,185,80,0.08)' : 'rgba(248,81,73,0.08)',\n          fill: true, tension: 0.3, pointRadius: 0, borderWidth: 2,\n        },\n        {\n          label: 'Starting Balance',\n          data: labels.map(() => startVal),\n          borderColor: '#30363d', borderDash: [5,5], pointRadius: 0, borderWidth: 1,\n        }\n      ]\n    },\n    options: {\n      responsive: true,\n      animation: false,\n      interaction: { mode: 'index', intersect: false },\n      plugins: {\n        legend: { labels: { color: '#8b949e', font: { size: 11 } } },\n        tooltip: {\n          backgroundColor: '#161b22', borderColor: '#30363d', borderWidth: 1,\n          titleColor: '#c9d1d9', bodyColor: '#e1e4e8',\n          callbacks: { label: ctx => '$' + ctx.parsed.y.toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2}) }\n        }\n      },\n      scales: {\n        x: { ticks: { color: '#8b949e', maxTicksLimit: 10 }, grid: { color: '#21262d' } },\n        y: { ticks: { color: '#8b949e', callback: v => '$' + v.toLocaleString() }, grid: { color: '#21262d' } }\n      }\n    }\n  });\n}\n\n// --- Init ---\nconst savedMode = localStorage.getItem('dashboardMode') || 'simple';\nsetMode(savedMode);\nfetchState();\nsetInterval(fetchState, 5000);  // poll every 5 seconds\n</script>\n</body>\n</html>\"\"\"\n",

meta.textContent = `${(s.exchange||'').toUpperCase()} | ${s.strategy || '--'} | ${s.timeframe || '--'} | Tick #${s.tick || 0}`;\\n\\n  // Price ticker\\n  updateTicker(s.prices || {});\\n\\n  // Portfolio cards\\n  updatePortfolioCards(s.portfolio || {});\\n\\n  // Positions\\n  updatePositions(s.portfolio?.positions || {});\\n\\n  // Signals (advanced)\\n  updateSignals(s.signals || []);\\n\\n  // Trade log\\n  updateTrades(s.trade_log || []);\\n\\n  // Equity chart (advanced)\\n  updateEquityChart(s.equity_curve || []);\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateTicker(prices) {\\n  const el = document.getElementById('ticker');\\n  if (!Object.keys(prices).length) { el.innerHTML = '<div class=\\\"ticker-item\\\"><span class=\\\"meta-text\\\">Waiting for price data...</span></div>'; return; }\\n  el.innerHTML = Object.entries(prices).map(([pair, price]) =>\\n    `<div class=\\\"ticker-item\\\">\\n      <span class=\\\"ticker-pair\\\">${pair}</span>\\n      <span class=\\\"ticker-price\\\">$${Number(price).toLocaleString(undefined, {minimumFractionDigits:2, maximumFractionDigits:2})}</span>\\n    </div>`\\n  ).join('');\\n}\\n\\nfunction updatePortfolioCards(p) {\\n  const pnlClass = (p.total_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative';\\n  const pnlSign = (p.total_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? '+' : '';\\n\\n  let html = `\\n    <div class=\\\"card\\\">\\n      <div class=\\\"card-label\\\">Portfolio Value</div>\\n      <div class=\\\"card-value neutral\\\">$${(p.total_value || 0).toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2})}</div>\\n    </div>\\n    <div class=\\\"card\\\">\\n      <div class=\\\"card-label\\\">P&L</div>\\n      <div class=\\\"card-value ${pnlClass}\\\">${pnlSign}$${(p.total_pnl || 0).toFixed(2)}</div>\\n      <div class=\\\"card-sub\\\">${pnlSign}${(p.total_pnl_pct || 0).toFixed(2)}%</div>\\n    </div>\\n    <div class=\\\"card\\\">\\n      <div class=\\\"card-label\\\">Cash</div>\\n      <div class=\\\"card-value neutral\\\">$${(p.cash || 0).toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2})}</div>\\n    </div>\\n    <div class=\\\"card\\\">\\n      <div class=\\\"card-label\\\">Open Positions</div>\\n      <div class=\\\"card-value neutral\\\">${p.open_positions || 0}</div>\\n    </div>`;\\n\\n  // Advanced-only cards\\n  html += `\\n    <div class=\\\"card advanced-only\\\">\\n      <div class=\\\"card-label\\\">Total Trades</div>\\n      <div class=\\\"card-value neutral\\\">${p.total_trades || 0}</div>\\n    </div>`;\\n\\n  document.getElementById('portfolioCards').innerHTML = html;\\n}\\n\\nfunction updatePositions(positions) {\\n  const section = document.getElementById('positionsSection');\\n  const body = document.getElementById('positionsBody');\\n  const entries = Object.entries(positions);\\n\\n  if (!entries.length) { section.style.display = 'none'; return; }\\n  section.style.display = 'block';\\n\\n  body.innerHTML = entries.map(([pair, pos]) => {\\n    const pnlClass = (pos.unrealized_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? 'positive' : 'negative';\\n    const sign = (pos.unrealized_pnl || 0) >= 0 ? '+' : '';\\n    return `<tr>\\n      <td><strong>${pair}</strong></td>\\n      <td>${pos.quantity}</td>\\n      <td>$${(pos.entry_price||0).toLocaleString()}</td>\\n      <td>$${(pos.current_price||0).toLocaleString()}</td>\\n      <td class=\\\"${pnlClass}\\\">${sign}$${(pos.unrealized_pnl||0).toFixed(2)} (${sign}${(pos.unrealized_pnl_pct||0).toFixed(2)}%)</td>\\n    </tr>`;\\n  }).join('');\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateSignals(signals) {\\n  const body = document.getElementById('signalsBody');\\n  const recent = signals.slice(-15).reverse();\\n  body.innerHTML = recent.map(s => {\\n    const badgeClass = s.signal === 'BUY' ? 'badge-buy' : s.signal === 'SELL' ? 'badge-sell' : 'badge-hold';\\n    return `<tr>\\n      <td>${(s.time||'').slice(11,19)}</td>\\n      <td>${s.pair}</td>\\n      <td><span class=\\\"badge ${badgeClass}\\\">${s.signal}</span></td>\\n      <td>${(s.confidence * 100).toFixed(0)}%</td>\\n      <td style=\\\"font-size:11px;color:#8b949e;max-width:300px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap\\\">${s.reason}</td>\\n    </tr>`;\\n  }).join('');\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateTrades(trades) {\\n  const body = document.getElementById('tradesBody');\\n  if (!trades.length) {\\n    body.innerHTML = '<tr><td colspan=\\\"6\\\" style=\\\"color:#8b949e;text-align:center\\\">No trades yet \\u2014 waiting for signals...</td></tr>';\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  const recent = trades.slice(-20).reverse();\\n  body.innerHTML = recent.map(t => {\\n    const badgeClass = t.side === 'BUY' ? 'badge-buy' : 'badge-sell';\\n    return `<tr>\\n      <td>${(t.time||'').slice(11,19)}</td>\\n      <td>${t.pair}</td>\\n      <td><span class=\\\"badge ${badgeClass}\\\">${t.side}</span></td>\\n      <td>$${(t.price||0).toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2})}</td>\\n      <td>$${(t.cost||0).toFixed(2)}</td>\\n      <td style=\\\"font-size:11px;color:#8b949e;max-width:250px;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap\\\">${t.reason||''}</td>\\n    </tr>`;\\n  }).join('');\\n}\\n\\nfunction updateEquityChart(curve) {\\n  const canvas = document.getElementById('equityChart');\\n  if (!curve.length) return;\\n\\n  const labels = curve.map(p => p.time ? p.time.slice(11, 19) : '');\\n  const data = curve.map(p => p.value);\\n  const startVal = data[0] || 10000;\\n  const isPositive = data[data.length - 1] >= startVal;\\n\\n  if (equityChart) {\\n    equityChart.data.labels = labels;\\n    equityChart.data.datasets[0].data = data;\\n    equityChart.data.datasets[0].borderColor = isPositive ? '#3fb950' : '#f85149';\\n    equityChart.data.datasets[0].backgroundColor = isPositive ? 'rgba(63,185,80,0.08)' : 'rgba(248,81,73,0.08)';\\n    equityChart.data.datasets[1].data = labels.map(() => startVal);\\n    equityChart.update('none');\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  equityChart = new Chart(canvas, {\\n    type: 'line',\\n    data: {\\n      labels,\\n      datasets: [\\n        {\\n          label: 'Portfolio ($)',\\n          data,\\n          borderColor: isPositive ? '#3fb950' : '#f85149',\\n          backgroundColor: isPositive ? 'rgba(63,185,80,0.08)' : 'rgba(248,81,73,0.08)',\\n          fill: true, tension: 0.3, pointRadius: 0, borderWidth: 2,\\n        },\\n        {\\n          label: 'Starting Balance',\\n          data: labels.map(() => startVal),\\n          borderColor: '#30363d', borderDash: [5,5], pointRadius: 0, borderWidth: 1,\\n        }\\n      ]\\n    },\\n    options: {\\n      responsive: true,\\n      animation: false,\\n      interaction: { mode: 'index', intersect: false },\\n      plugins: {\\n        legend: { labels: { color: '#8b949e', font: { size: 11 } } },\\n        tooltip: {\\n          backgroundColor: '#161b22', borderColor: '#30363d', borderWidth: 1,\\n          titleColor: '#c9d1d9', bodyColor: '#e1e4e8',\\n          callbacks: { label: ctx => '$' + ctx.parsed.y.toLocaleString(undefined,{minimumFractionDigits:2}) }\\n        }\\n      },\\n      scales: {\\n        x: { ticks: { color: '#8b949e', maxTicksLimit: 10 }, grid: { color: '#21262d' } },\\n        y: { ticks: { color: '#8b949e', callback: v => '$' + v.toLocaleString() }, grid: { color: '#21262d' } }\\n      }\\n    }\\n  });\\n}\\n\\n// --- Init ---\\nconst savedMode = localStorage.getItem('dashboardMode') || 'simple';\\nsetMode(savedMode);\\nfetchState();\\nsetInterval(fetchState, 5000);  // poll every 5 seconds\\n</script>\\n</body>\\n</html>\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/crypto-trading-agent/src/agent/server.py\"}"

      "content": "# Unusual Whales' MCP server won't power your crypto bot\n\n**The tweet announces Unusual Whales' MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, which lets AI tools like Claude, Cursor, and Codex query 100+ financial data endpoints in natural language \u2014 but it's almost entirely focused on U.S. equities and options, making it a poor fit for a crypto trading agent.** The platform's real strength is options flow, dark pool data, and congressional trading activity, none of which translate meaningfully to cryptocurrency markets. Your ccxt/Kraken setup already delivers the core data these strategies need, and far better crypto-specific supplements exist.\n\n## What the tweet actually says\n\nThe tweet from @unusual_whales (status ID 2032214752395739302) reads: *\"Grab your API key, connect the MCP server, and start building in minutes\"* with a link to their MCP setup guide at `unusualwhales.com/public-api/mcp`. It targets developers building with AI coding tools \u2014 Claude, OpenClaw, Codex, Cursor, Perplexity \u2014 offering a plug-and-play connection to Unusual Whales' financial data. The MCP server is available as a remote hosted endpoint or a local NPM package (`@unusualwhales/mcp`), with **30+ pre-built analysis prompts** covering options flow, dark pool activity, Greek exposure, volatility, and stock fundamentals. The announcement positions Unusual Whales as infrastructure for AI-assisted financial analysis, specifically courting the \"vibecoder\" and professional developer audience.\n\n## Unusual Whales is an options flow powerhouse, not a crypto platform\n\nUnusual Whales launched in 2021 as an options analytics platform and has grown into one of the most comprehensive retail-facing market data services for U.S. equities. Its core product is a **real-time options flow feed** tracking every options trade across all U.S. exchanges, filterable by expiration, volume, trade type (sweeps, blocks), and Greek exposure. The platform also offers dark pool prints, congressional trading trackers (they created the NANC and KRUZ ETFs mirroring politician portfolios), institutional 13F filings, insider transactions, gamma exposure charts, and implied volatility analytics.\n\nThe API is well-built \u2014 **100+ REST endpoints**, WebSocket streaming, Kafka for enterprise, and two Python client libraries on PyPI. Pricing starts around **$48\u201350/month** for individual traders, with enterprise tiers for historical data and streaming. The MCP server announced in the tweet represents their strategic push into AI/LLM integration, making it easy for AI agents to query financial data conversationally.\n\nHowever, crypto is an afterthought. Only **4 of 100+ API endpoints** are crypto-related: whale transactions, recent whale trades, OHLC candles, and pair state. That's it. No crypto options flow, no on-chain analytics, no DeFi data, no exchange-specific order flow.\n\n## Why this doesn't fit your trading agent\n\nYour crypto trading agent uses SMA crossovers, RSI, MACD, and Bollinger Bands on data pulled from Kraken via ccxt. Here's the mismatch with Unusual Whales:\n\n**Dark pool data is irrelevant to crypto.** Dark pools are SEC-regulated Alternative Trading Systems for off-exchange equity block trades. Crypto exchanges use transparent order books \u2014 the concept doesn't apply. Unusual Whales' marquee dark pool product has zero crypto utility.\n\n**Options flow tracks U.S. equity options, not crypto options.** The crypto options market lives primarily on **Deribit** (which handles the vast majority of BTC/ETH options volume). Unusual Whales does not track Deribit or any crypto options venue. The only indirect signal would be monitoring options flow on crypto-adjacent equities like MSTR, COIN, or Bitcoin ETFs (IBIT, BITO), but that's a weak, lagging proxy for actual crypto price action.\n\n**OHLC data is redundant.** ccxt already pulls OHLC candles directly from Kraken with full granularity. Unusual Whales' crypto OHLC endpoint adds nothing your setup doesn't already have. The technical indicators your strategies compute (SMA, RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands) all derive from OHLC data you're already sourcing.\n\n**The one potentially useful feature** is crypto whale transaction tracking \u2014 monitoring unusually large trades across exchanges. But with only two endpoints dedicated to this, the data is shallow compared to purpose-built alternatives.\n\n## Better tools to supplement your ccxt/Kraken stack\n\nIf you want additive signals beyond the technical analysis indicators you're already computing, these crypto-native platforms provide data that actually complements your setup:\n\n- **Whale Alert** (~$49/month) delivers real-time large transaction tracking across 10+ blockchains with a REST and WebSocket API \u2014 the best-in-class option for detecting whale movements that could precede price swings\n- **Santiment** offers on-chain metrics, social sentiment, and developer activity for 2,000+ assets through a Python client (`sanpy`) that returns Pandas DataFrames, integrating cleanly with TA pipelines\n- **CoinGlass** provides crypto derivatives data including open interest, liquidation levels, and funding rates from Deribit, Binance, and other venues \u2014 useful if you want to layer derivatives sentiment onto your technical signals\n- **Glassnode** delivers institutional-grade on-chain analytics (MVRV, exchange flows, accumulation trends) for Bitcoin and Ethereum\n\nEach of these provides genuinely novel data streams \u2014 whale movements, on-chain accumulation patterns, social sentiment spikes, derivatives positioning \u2014 that your SMA/RSI/MACD/Bollinger strategy cannot capture from price data alone.\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe Unusual Whales MCP server is an impressive piece of developer infrastructure for anyone building AI-powered tools around U.S. equity and options markets. The ability to query options flow, dark pool activity, and congressional trading data through natural language via Claude or Cursor is genuinely powerful for that domain. But **96% of their data has no bearing on crypto markets**, and the 4% that does is either redundant with what ccxt/Kraken already provides or too shallow to justify a subscription. Your development time is better spent integrating Whale Alert or Santiment for signals that complement \u2014 rather than duplicate \u2014 your existing technical analysis stack.",

Register it in STRATEGY_REGISTRY below\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom typing import Any\\n\\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.sma_crossover import SMACrossoverStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.rsi import RSIStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.macd import MACDStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.bollinger_bands import BollingerBandsStrategy\\n\\n# Add new strategies here\\nSTRATEGY_REGISTRY: dict[str, type[BaseStrategy]] = {\\n    \\\"sma_crossover\\\": SMACrossoverStrategy,\\n    \\\"rsi\\\": RSIStrategy,\\n    \\\"macd\\\": MACDStrategy,\\n    \\\"bollinger_bands\\\": BollingerBandsStrategy,\\n}\\n\\n\\ndef load_strategy(name: str, params: dict[str, Any]) -> BaseStrategy:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Instantiate a strategy by its config name.\\n\\n    Parameters\\n    ----------\\n    name : str\\n        The strategy name from config.yaml (e.g. \\\"sma_crossover\\\").\\n    params : dict\\n        Strategy-specific parameters.\\n\\n    Returns\\n    -------\\n    BaseStrategy\\n\\n    Raises\\n    ------\\n    ValueError\\n        If the strategy name isn't registered.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    cls = STRATEGY_REGISTRY.get(name)\\n    if cls is None:\\n        available = \\\", \\\".join(STRATEGY_REGISTRY.keys())\\n        raise ValueError(f\\\"Unknown strategy: '{name}'.       "description": "Create the consensus strategy \u2014 runs all 4 strategies and trades when multiple agree",
      "file_text": "\"\"\"\nConsensus Strategy \u2014 runs ALL strategies and trades when multiple agree.\n\nInstead of relying on a single strategy, the consensus approach runs\nSMA Crossover, RSI, MACD, and Bollinger Bands on every tick. It only\nexecutes a trade when a configurable number of strategies agree on the\nsame signal.\n\nThis reduces false signals and increases confidence in each trade.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pandas as pd\nimport structlog\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\nfrom agent.strategies.sma_crossover import SMACrossoverStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.rsi import RSIStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.macd import MACDStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.bollinger_bands import BollingerBandsStrategy\n\nlog = structlog.get_logger()\n\n\nclass ConsensusStrategy(BaseStrategy):\n    \"\"\"\n    Meta-strategy that runs all sub-strategies and trades on agreement.\n\n    Config params:\n        min_agree (int): Minimum strategies that must agree. Default 2.\n        fast_period (int): SMA fast period. Default 5 (tuned for 5m candles).\n        slow_period (int): SMA slow period. Default 15.\n        rsi_period (int): RSI lookback. Default 14.\n        rsi_oversold (float): RSI buy threshold. Default 35.\n        rsi_overbought (float): RSI sell threshold. Default 65.\n        macd_fast (int): MACD fast EMA. Default 8.\n        macd_slow (int): MACD slow EMA. Default 21.\n        macd_signal (int): MACD signal period. Default 5.\n        bb_period (int): Bollinger Bands period. Default 15.\n        bb_std (float): Bollinger Bands std dev. Default 2.0.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        super().__init__(params)\n        self.min_agree = params.get(\"min_agree\", 2)\n\n        # Build sub-strategies with tuned params for short timeframes\n        self._strategies: list[BaseStrategy] = [\n            SMACrossoverStrategy({\n                \"fast_period\": params.get(\"fast_period\", 5),\n                \"slow_period\": params.get(\"slow_period\", 15),\n            }),\n            RSIStrategy({\n                \"period\": params.get(\"rsi_period\", 14),\n                \"oversold\": params.get(\"rsi_oversold\", 35),\n                \"overbought\": params.get(\"rsi_overbought\", 65),\n            }),\n            MACDStrategy({\n                \"fast_period\": params.get(\"macd_fast\", 8),\n                \"slow_period\": params.get(\"macd_slow\", 21),\n                \"signal_period\": params.get(\"macd_signal\", 5),\n            }),\n            BollingerBandsStrategy({\n                \"period\": params.get(\"bb_period\", 15),\n                \"std_dev\": params.get(\"bb_std\", 2.0),\n            }),\n        ]\n\n    @property\n    def name(self) -> str:\n        return \"consensus\"\n\n    @property\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\n        return max(s.required_history for s in self._strategies) + 5\n\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\n        # Run every sub-strategy\n        results: list[TradeRecommendation] = []\n        for strategy in self._strategies:\n            try:\n                rec = strategy.evaluate(pair, candles)\n                results.append(rec)\n            except Exception as e:\n                log.warning(\"consensus.sub_strategy_error\", strategy=strategy.name, error=str(e))\n\n        if not results:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\n                confidence=0.0,\n                reason=\"No sub-strategies returned results\",\n                metadata={},\n            )\n\n        # Count votes\n        buy_votes: list[str] = []\n        sell_votes: list[str] = []\n        hold_votes: list[str] = []\n        all_reasons: list[str] = []\n        total_confidence = 0.0\n\n        strategy_details: dict[str, Any] = {}\n\n        for rec in results:\n            strategy_name = rec.metadata.get(\"strategy_name\", rec.reason[:20])\n            # Try to identify which strategy produced this\n            for s in self._strategies:\n                if s.evaluate.__qualname__.split(\".\")[0] in type(s).__name__:\n                    strategy_name = s.name\n                    break\n\n            if rec.signal == Signal.BUY:\n                buy_votes.append(rec.reason)\n                total_confidence += rec.confidence\n            elif rec.signal == Signal.SELL:\n                sell_votes.append(rec.reason)\n                total_confidence += rec.confidence\n            else:\n                hold_votes.append(rec.reason)\n\n            all_reasons.append(f\"{rec.signal.value}({rec.confidence:.0%})\")\n\n        # Build detailed metadata for the dashboard\n        vote_summary = []\n        for i, rec in enumerate(results):\n            s_name = self._strategies[i].name if i < len(self._strategies) else f\"strategy_{i}\"\n            vote_summary.append(f\"{s_name}={rec.signal.value}\")\n            strategy_details[s_name] = {\n                \"signal\": rec.signal.value,\n                \"confidence\": rec.confidence,\n                \"reason\": rec.reason,\n            }\n\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"buy_count\": len(buy_votes),\n            \"sell_count\": len(sell_votes),\n            \"hold_count\": len(hold_votes),\n            \"min_agree\": self.min_agree,\n            \"votes\": \", \".join(vote_summary),\n            \"strategies\": strategy_details,\n            \"price\": float(candles[\"close\"].iloc[-1]),\n        }\n\n        # Decide: BUY if enough strategies agree\n        if len(buy_votes) >= self.min_agree:\n            avg_confidence = total_confidence / len(results)\n            agreement_bonus = len(buy_votes) / len(results)  # higher when more agree\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.5 + agreement_bonus * 0.5)\n\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"CONSENSUS BUY: {len(buy_votes)}/{len(results)} agree [{', '.join(vote_summary)}]\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # SELL if enough strategies agree\n        if len(sell_votes) >= self.min_agree:\n            avg_confidence = total_confidence / len(results)\n            agreement_bonus = len(sell_votes) / len(results)\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.5 + agreement_bonus * 0.5)\n\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"CONSENSUS SELL: {len(sell_votes)}/{len(results)} agree [{', '.join(vote_summary)}]\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # No consensus \u2014 hold\n        return TradeRecommendation(\n            pair=pair,\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\n            confidence=0.3,\n            reason=f\"No consensus: {len(buy_votes)}B/{len(sell_votes)}S/{len(hold_votes)}H [{', '.join(vote_summary)}]\",\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n",

It only\\nexecutes a trade when a configurable number of strategies agree on the\\nsame signal.\\n\\nThis reduces false signals and increases confidence in each trade.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom typing import Any\\n\\nimport pandas as pd\\nimport structlog\\n\\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\\nfrom agent.strategies.sma_crossover import SMACrossoverStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.rsi import RSIStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.macd import MACDStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.bollinger_bands import BollingerBandsStrategy\\n\\nlog = structlog.get_logger()\\n\\n\\nclass ConsensusStrategy(BaseStrategy):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Meta-strategy that runs all sub-strategies and trades on agreement.\\n\\n    Config params:\\n        min_agree (int): Minimum strategies that must agree. Default 2.\\n        fast_period (int): SMA fast period. Default 5 (tuned for 5m candles).\\n        slow_period (int): SMA slow period. Default 15.\\n        rsi_period (int): RSI lookback. Default 14.\\n        rsi_oversold (float): RSI buy threshold. Default 35.\\n        rsi_overbought (float): RSI sell threshold. Default 65.\\n        macd_fast (int): MACD fast EMA. Default 8.\\n        macd_slow (int): MACD slow EMA. Default 21.\\n        macd_signal (int): MACD signal period. Default 5.\\n        bb_period (int): Bollinger Bands period. Default 15.\\n        bb_std (float): Bollinger Bands std dev. Default 2.0.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n        super().__init__(params)\\n        self.min_agree = params.get(\\\"min_agree\\\", 2)\\n\\n        # Build sub-strategies with tuned params for short timeframes\\n        self._strategies: list[BaseStrategy] = [\\n            SMACrossoverStrategy({\\n                \\\"fast_period\\\": params.get(\\\"fast_period\\\", 5),\\n                \\\"slow_period\\\": params.get(\\\"slow_period\\\", 15),\\n            }),\\n            RSIStrategy({\\n                \\\"period\\\": params.get(\\\"rsi_period\\\", 14),\\n                \\\"oversold\\\": params.get(\\\"rsi_oversold\\\", 35),\\n                \\\"overbought\\\": params.get(\\\"rsi_overbought\\\", 65),\\n            }),\\n            MACDStrategy({\\n                \\\"fast_period\\\": params.get(\\\"macd_fast\\\", 8),\\n                \\\"slow_period\\\": params.get(\\\"macd_slow\\\", 21),\\n                \\\"signal_period\\\": params.get(\\\"macd_signal\\\", 5),\\n            }),\\n            BollingerBandsStrategy({\\n                \\\"period\\\": params.get(\\\"bb_period\\\", 15),\\n                \\\"std_dev\\\": params.get(\\\"bb_std\\\", 2.0),\\n            }),\\n        ]\\n\\n    @property\\n    def name(self) -> str:\\n        return \\\"consensus\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\\n        return max(s.required_history for s in self._strategies) + 5\\n\\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\\n        # Run every sub-strategy\\n        results: list[TradeRecommendation] = []\\n        for strategy in self._strategies:\\n            try:\\n                rec = strategy.evaluate(pair, candles)\\n                results.append(rec)\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                log.warning(\\\"consensus.sub_strategy_error\\\", strategy=strategy.name, error=str(e))\\n\\n        if not results:\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\\n                confidence=0.0,\\n                reason=\\\"No sub-strategies returned results\\\",\\n                metadata={},\\n            )\\n\\n        # Count votes\\n        buy_votes: list[str] = []\\n        sell_votes: list[str] = []\\n        hold_votes: list[str] = []\\n        all_reasons: list[str] = []\\n        total_confidence = 0.0\\n\\n        strategy_details: dict[str, Any] = {}\\n\\n        for rec in results:\\n            strategy_name = rec.metadata.get(\\\"strategy_name\\\", rec.reason[:20])\\n            # Try to identify which strategy produced this\\n            for s in self._strategies:\\n                if s.evaluate.__qualname__.split(\\\".\\\")[0] in type(s).__name__:\\n                    strategy_name = s.name\\n                    break\\n\\n            if rec.signal == Signal.BUY:\\n                buy_votes.append(rec.reason)\\n                total_confidence += rec.confidence\\n            elif rec.signal == Signal.SELL:\\n                sell_votes.append(rec.reason)\\n                total_confidence += rec.confidence\\n            else:\\n                hold_votes.append(rec.reason)\\n\\n            all_reasons.append(f\\\"{rec.signal.value}({rec.confidence:.0%})\\\")\\n\\n        # Build detailed metadata for the dashboard\\n        vote_summary = []\\n        for i, rec in enumerate(results):\\n            s_name = self._strategies[i].name if i < len(self._strategies) else f\\\"strategy_{i}\\\"\\n            vote_summary.append(f\\\"{s_name}={rec.signal.value}\\\")\\n            strategy_details[s_name] = {\\n                \\\"signal\\\": rec.signal.value,\\n                \\\"confidence\\\": rec.confidence,\\n                \\\"reason\\\": rec.reason,\\n            }\\n\\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\\n            \\\"buy_count\\\": len(buy_votes),\\n            \\\"sell_count\\\": len(sell_votes),\\n            \\\"hold_count\\\": len(hold_votes),\\n            \\\"min_agree\\\": self.min_agree,\\n            \\\"votes\\\": \\\", \\\".join(vote_summary),\\n            \\\"strategies\\\": strategy_details,\\n            \\\"price\\\": float(candles[\\\"close\\\"].iloc[-1]),\\n        }\\n\\n        # Decide: BUY if enough strategies agree\\n        if len(buy_votes) >= self.min_agree:\\n            avg_confidence = total_confidence / len(results)\\n            agreement_bonus = len(buy_votes) / len(results)  # higher when more agree\\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.5 + agreement_bonus * 0.5)\\n\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\\n                reason=f\\\"CONSENSUS BUY: {len(buy_votes)}/{len(results)} agree [{', '.join(vote_summary)}]\\\",\\n                metadata=metadata,\\n            )\\n\\n        # SELL if enough strategies agree\\n        if len(sell_votes) >= self.min_agree:\\n            avg_confidence = total_confidence / len(results)\\n            agreement_bonus = len(sell_votes) / len(results)\\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.5 + agreement_bonus * 0.5)\\n\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\\n                reason=f\\\"CONSENSUS SELL: {len(sell_votes)}/{len(results)} agree [{', '.join(vote_summary)}]\\\",\\n                metadata=metadata,\\n            )\\n\\n        # No consensus \\u2014 hold\\n        return TradeRecommendation(\\n            pair=pair,\\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\\n            confidence=0.3,\\n            reason=f\\\"No consensus: {len(buy_votes)}B/{len(sell_votes)}S/{len(hold_votes)}H [{', '.join(vote_summary)}]\\\",\\n            metadata=metadata,\\n        )\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/crypto-trading-agent/src/agent/strategies/consensus.py\"}"
    },
    "approval_options": null,
    "approval_key": null,

      "file_text": "\"\"\"Tests for the consensus strategy.\"\"\"\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import Signal\nfrom agent.strategies.consensus import ConsensusStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.loader import load_strategy\n\n\ndef _make_candles(prices: list[float]) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    n = len(prices)\n    return pd.DataFrame(\n        {\n            \"open\": prices,\n            \"high\": [p * 1.01 for p in prices],\n            \"low\": [p * 0.99 for p in prices],\n            \"close\": prices,\n            \"volume\": [1000.0] * n,\n        },\n        index=pd.date_range(\"2025-01-01\", periods=n, freq=\"5min\", tz=\"UTC\"),\n    )\n\n\ndef test_consensus_returns_hold_on_flat_data():\n    \"\"\"Flat prices should produce HOLD \u2014 no strategy should signal.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\"min_agree\": 2})\n    prices = [100.0] * 50\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n    assert result.signal == Signal.HOLD\n    assert \"votes\" in result.metadata\n\n\ndef test_consensus_metadata_has_vote_counts():\n    \"\"\"Metadata should include buy/sell/hold vote counts.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\"min_agree\": 2})\n    prices = [100.0] * 50\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    assert \"buy_count\" in result.metadata\n    assert \"sell_count\" in result.metadata\n    assert \"hold_count\" in result.metadata\n    assert result.metadata[\"buy_count\"] + result.metadata[\"sell_count\"] + result.metadata[\"hold_count\"] == 4\n\n\ndef test_consensus_buy_when_multiple_agree():\n    \"\"\"When price crashes hard, RSI + Bollinger should both say BUY.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\n        \"min_agree\": 2,\n        \"rsi_period\": 14,\n        \"rsi_oversold\": 35,\n        \"bb_period\": 15,\n        \"bb_std\": 2.0,\n        \"fast_period\": 3,\n        \"slow_period\": 5,\n    })\n    # Steady prices then a big crash \u2014 should trigger multiple buy signals\n    prices = [100.0] * 40 + [70.0]  # massive drop\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    # At minimum, the metadata should show strategy votes\n    assert result.metadata[\"buy_count\"] >= 0\n    assert \"votes\" in result.metadata\n\n\ndef test_consensus_sell_when_multiple_agree():\n    \"\"\"When price spikes hard, RSI + Bollinger should both say SELL.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\n        \"min_agree\": 2,\n        \"rsi_period\": 14,\n        \"rsi_overbought\": 65,\n        \"bb_period\": 15,\n        \"bb_std\": 2.0,\n        \"fast_period\": 3,\n        \"slow_period\": 5,\n    })\n    prices = [100.0] * 40 + [130.0]  # massive spike\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    assert result.metadata[\"sell_count\"] >= 0\n    assert \"votes\" in result.metadata\n\n\ndef test_consensus_requires_min_agree():\n    \"\"\"With min_agree=4, it should almost never trade \u2014 very conservative.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\"min_agree\": 4})\n    prices = [100.0] * 50\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n    # 4/4 agreement on flat data is virtually impossible for a non-HOLD signal\n    assert result.signal == Signal.HOLD\n\n\ndef test_consensus_loadable_by_name():\n    \"\"\"Consensus     "text": "Your ensemble trading strategies research report is ready. It covers five decades of forecast combination evidence, the forecast combination puzzle (why simple equal weights beat optimized weights), practical implementation approaches from hard consensus to soft scoring, cryptocurrency-specific findings, and critical overfitting risks including Novy-Marx's power law for multi-signal bias. The report synthesizes academic research from Bates & Granger through 2025, practitioner evidence from Goldman Sachs, Man Group, and Quantopian, and concludes with actionable principles for building robust signal combination systems.",
    "citations": []
  },
  {
    "start_timestamp": null,
    "stop_timestamp": null,
    "flags": null,
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": null,
    "name": "artifacts",
    "input": {
      "id": "compass_artifact_wf-349904a5-a288-4626-ac81-b78cd9c6a7da_text/markdown",
      "type": "text/markdown",
      "title": "Ensemble Trading Strategies: Strong Theory, Treacherous Practice",
      "source": "c",
      "command": "create",
      "content": "# Ensemble trading strategies: strong theory, treacherous practice\n\n**Combining multiple trading strategies through weighted scoring or voting is one of the most well-supported ideas in quantitative finance \u2014 yet it is also one of the easiest to implement badly.** Over 50 years of forecast combination research, anchored by Bates & Granger's 1969 seminal paper, demonstrates that combining imperfectly correlated signals consistently reduces error and improves risk-adjusted returns. The practical evidence from quantitative trading confirms this: factor-diversified portfolios roughly triple the Sharpe ratio of asset-class-diversified portfolios (from ~0.48 to ~1.44 in Ilmanen & Kizer's landmark 2012 study). But the same literature carries a critical warning \u2014 **simple equal-weighted averaging of diverse strategies almost always outperforms complex weight optimization**, and the more parameters you tune in an ensemble, the more likely you are to overfit catastrophically. For cryptocurrency markets specifically, ensembles show even greater promise due to higher volatility and market inefficiency, but face amplified overfitting risk from shorter data histories and more extreme regime shifts.\n\n## Five decades of evidence favor signal combination\n\nThe academic case for ensemble approaches rests on three converging pillars. First, the **forecast combination literature** \u2014 beginning with Bates & Granger (1969) and comprehensively reviewed by Clemen (1989) across 209 studies \u2014 has produced a virtually unanimous conclusion: combining multiple forecasts leads to increased accuracy. Clemen's summary remains definitive: \"The results have been virtually unanimous: combining multiple forecasts leads to increased forecast accuracy.\" Wang, Hyndman, Li & Kang's 2023 review in the *International Journal of Forecasting*, covering 50+ years of research, reconfirmed this finding.\n\nSecond, **equity premium forecasting research** provides direct financial-market evidence. Rapach, Strauss & Zhou (2010) in the *Review of Financial Studies* showed that while individual predictive models based on single macroeconomic variables failed to beat a historical average out-of-sample, combining those same forecasts delivered statistically and economically significant gains. Neely, Rapach, Tu & Zhou (2014) in *Management Science* extended this by demonstrating that combining technical indicators with macroeconomic variables via principal components produced what they called \"the best monthly U.S. equity premium forecast\" \u2014 because technicals and macro variables provide complementary information across business cycles.\n\nThird, the **strategy diversification literature** provides the portfolio-construction angle. Bailey & L\u00f3pez de Prado (2012) proved mathematically that multi-strategy portfolios can dramatically improve Sharpe ratios, and that it is sometimes rational to add strategies with low or even negative standalone Sharpe ratios if their correlation with existing strategies is sufficiently low. Man Group's 2025 research demonstrated that a multi-strategy portfolio \"almost tripled the return and Sharpe ratio with a third of the maximum drawdown\" relative to a 60/40 benchmark over 2006\u20132024.\n\n## Simple weights beat \"optimal\" weights \u2014 the forecast combination puzzle\n\nPerhaps the most counterintuitive and practically important finding in this literature is the **forecast combination puzzle**, coined by Stock & Watson (2004): simple equal-weighted averages consistently outperform sophisticated adaptive combination methods. Smith & Wallis (2009) and Claeskens, Magnus, Vasnev & Wang (2016) provided the theoretical explanation \u2014 estimation error in determining \"optimal\" weights is large enough to dominate any theoretical gains from optimized weighting. Equal weights, having zero estimation error, win by default.\n\nThis has direct implications for practitioners building weighted scoring systems. Goldman Sachs Asset Management's research on signal combination found that differences between \"mixed\" (normalize then combine) and \"integrated\" (combine raw signals) approaches were **\"economically negligible\"** \u2014 the combination method mattered far less than signal quality and diversity. Richmond Quantitative Advisors' 2020 study of 110 momentum strategy variants found that an equal-weighted \"Momentum Ensemble\" consistently performed in the **top 10\u201315%** on a risk-adjusted basis, despite using no optimization whatsoever. The ensemble eliminated curve-fitting risk by definition.\n\nThat said, dynamic weight adjustment is not entirely without merit. The FinRL framework's approach of applying a softmax function to rolling 30-day Sharpe ratios to weight reinforcement learning agents achieved the highest cumulative returns across multiple tests. A study using Information Coefficient-based weighting with 12-month rolling windows improved strategy returns from **16.65% to 39.09%** and Sharpe ratios from 0.24 to 0.61. The Numin weighted-majority ensemble research found that using utility (risk-adjusted returns) rather than raw accuracy as the scoring mechanism with a **25-minute lookback window** produced optimal results for intraday trading. The key pattern: dynamic weighting works when it is simple, uses short-to-medium lookback windows, and optimizes for risk-adjusted returns rather than raw performance.\n\n## Practical approaches span a clear complexity spectrum\n\nPractitioners combine trading signals using approaches that fall along a spectrum from simple hard consensus to complex adaptive scoring.\n\n**Hard consensus (N-out-of-M voting)** requires a threshold number of strategies to agree before executing. A typical implementation scores each indicator as +1 (buy) or 0 (neutral), sums the scores, and triggers a trade when the sum exceeds a threshold \u2014 for instance, requiring 3 of 5 indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, volume, trend) to show bullish before entering long. This approach is transparent, resistant to overfitting, and easy to debug, but discards signal-strength information.\n\n**Soft weighted scoring** assigns different weights to each indicator and creates a continuous composite score. Practical implementations range from fixed-weight systems (e.g., PSAR=3, Williams=4, RSI=4, with threshold levels for Low/Medium/High conviction) to fully adaptive systems. The SigmaKernel system on TradingView uses the formula `New_Weight = Current_Weight + (Component_Win_Rate - Average_Win_Rate) \u00d7 Adaptation_Speed`, creating a self-correcting mechanism where successful signal generators receive more influence. More sophisticated ML-enhanced systems track per-indicator prediction accuracy over rolling windows and weight accordingly.\n\n**Confidence-weighted voting** scales each vote by estimated reliability \u2014 a barely-triggered RSI is treated differently from a deeply oversold reading. Systems typically require minimum confidence thresholds (e.g., \u226570%) before executing, which reduces overtrading and transaction costs. Advanced ensemble architectures using meta-learning and stacking claim **5\u201315% performance improvements** over simple voting.\n\nThe practitioner consensus, reflected across sources from QuantConnect to Goldman Sachs, is clear: **start with hard consensus using orthogonal indicators, then graduate to weighted scoring only with sufficient data and robust out-of-sample testing.** The most important decision is not how you combine signals but which signals you combine. Mixing indicators from the same category (e.g., RSI + Stochastic + CCI \u2014 all momentum oscillators) provides duplicate information and creates false confidence, a pitfall called \"indicator redundancy.\" Effective ensembles combine trend (moving averages, ADX), momentum (RSI, MACD), volatility (Bollinger Bands, ATR), and volume (OBV, VWAP) indicators \u2014 **3 to 5 maximum** to avoid complexity explosion.\n\n## Cryptocurrency ensembles show amplified promise and peril\n\nFor crypto markets, ensemble methods appear particularly well-suited. A 2025 Frontiers in AI study found that an AI-driven ensemble achieved a **total return of 1,640%** on Bitcoin from 2018\u20132024, compared to 305% for a single ML model and 223% for buy-and-hold. During the 2022 bear market, the ensemble limited losses to **\u221235%** versus \u221265% for buy-and-hold. A Springer Nature study (2025) comparing models across 30 cryptocurrencies found that ensemble-based models (Gradient Boosting, XGBoost) consistently outperformed neural networks with R\u00b2 \u2248 0.98.\n\nCrypto's unique characteristics both help and hurt ensemble approaches. **Higher volatility** creates larger profit opportunities for multi-strategy approaches. **Market inefficiency** \u2014 well-documented in Kyriazis's 2019 survey \u2014 means more exploitable patterns exist for ensembles to capture. **Sentiment-driven dynamics** make multimodal ensembles combining technical, sentiment, and on-chain data particularly powerful; the SentiStack framework (2025) demonstrated \"superior robustness\" in capturing extreme Bitcoin price movements.\n\nHowever, crypto's **shorter data history** severely limits the statistical power of backtests, amplifying overfitting risk. **More extreme regime changes** \u2014 illustrated by Bitcoin's correlation with the S&P 500 shifting from ~0 to 0.47 in a single month (JPMorgan, 2021) \u2014 can destroy ensemble weight calibrations overnight. A notable QuantConnect practitioner with knowledge of successful hedge fund operations recommended the approach of building \"multiple very simple highly uncorrelated strategies\" with only 3\u20135 parameters each, then running all simultaneously with equal weights \u2014 explicitly avoiding regime prediction, which \"is probably more difficult than point estimates and no one is doing that in practice.\"\n\n## The overfitting trap deepens with every added signal\n\nThe risks of ensemble trading strategies are well-documented and severe, particularly for the weighted-scoring approach. **Novy-Marx's critical finding** quantifies the danger: the statistical bias from combining the best k signals from n candidates is almost as bad as selecting the single best from n^k candidates. A 3-signal ensemble drawn from 10 candidates has overfitting bias comparable to choosing the best of **1,000 candidates**. For a 5-signal ensemble from 20 candidates: equivalent to cherry-picking from 3.2 million. Novy-Marx demonstrated that combining randomly generated signals with zero true predictive power produces \"highly significant\" backtested performance \u2014 pure statistical illusion.\n\nQuantopian's landmark 888-strategy study (Wiecki et al., 2016, *Journal of Investing*) delivered perhaps the most sobering practical evidence: **backtested Sharpe ratios had essentially zero predictive power for out-of-sample performance** (R\u00b2 < 0.025). Worse, \"the more backtesting a quant has done for a strategy, the larger the discrepancy between backtest and out-of-sample performance.\" An AQR moving average strategy's Sharpe plummeted from **1.2 to \u22120.2** on fresh data. Across broader studies, 78% of published trading strategies fail out-of-sample, with average Sharpe ratios dropping 63%.\n\nFive specific pitfalls deserve attention:\n\n- **Correlation convergence under stress**: Technical analysis strategies derived from the same price data are inherently correlated, and this correlation spikes during crises \u2014 precisely when diversification is most needed. During the 2008 crisis, correlations across asset classes converged toward +1, and 68% of portfolio managers report experiencing unexpected correlation breakdowns.\n- **Look-ahead bias in weight selection**: Choosing which strategies to include or how to weight them based on full-period performance implicitly leaks future information into past decisions. Even walk-forward analysis can suffer when validation windows inform later selections.\n- **Regime-dependent weight decay**: Strategies optimized for trending markets fail in ranging markets. CFA Institute analysis (2026) documents how the 2020 and 2022 regime shifts caused complete breakdown of historical correlation matrices.\n- **Transaction cost amplification**: Multiple conflicting signals generate higher turnover. In crypto, wider spreads, higher exchange fees, and slippage in less liquid markets compound this problem. Expanding beyond ~20 cryptocurrencies introduces \"diminishing liquidity and higher transaction costs, potentially offsetting the benefits.\"\n- **False sense of security**: Ensemble methods are often marketed as a solution to overfitting, creating dangerous circular logic. Novy-Marx's power law shows multi-signal strategies can appear *more* statistically significant in backtests precisely because they are *more* overfit.\n\n## Hard consensus versus soft scoring: the verdict is nuanced\n\nThe evidence suggests that **soft scoring generally outperforms hard consensus in controlled academic settings**, but the margin is modest and comes with meaningfully higher overfitting risk. NYU Stern's meta-learning research found adaptive approaches with regularization \"consistently achieve superior risk-adjusted performance.\" The FinRL ensemble using softmax-weighted Sharpe ratios outperformed majority-voting ensembles. Quantpedia's research on dynamic signal-strength weighting confirms it as \"a widely utilized approach found to be effective.\"\n\nBut hard consensus has powerful practical advantages. It requires no weight optimization (eliminating the largest source of overfitting), is transparent and debuggable, and performs robustly across regimes because it makes fewer assumptions. The GSAM finding that combination-method differences are \"economically negligible\" suggests the performance gap is small enough that the robustness benefits of simplicity may dominate in practice.\n\nThe most effective practical approach, recommended across multiple sources, is **hybrid**: use hard consensus as a baseline filter for entry signals (require at least 2\u20133 of 5 orthogonal indicators to agree), then apply soft scoring for position sizing or confidence assessment. This captures most of the ensemble benefit while limiting parameter-optimization risk.\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe evidence for ensemble trading strategies is genuinely strong in theory and measurably real in practice \u2014 but only when implementation respects three principles the literature consistently identifies. First, **diversity of component strategies matters far more than the combination method**; combining correlated technical indicators provides illusory diversification. Second, **simple equal weighting is the correct default** \u2014 the forecast combination puzzle demonstrates that optimization of weights typically destroys more value through overfitting than it creates through precision. Third, **skepticism toward backtested ensemble performance should scale with complexity**; Novy-Marx's power law means that every signal added to an ensemble exponentially inflates the statistical threshold for genuine significance. For crypto markets specifically, the higher volatility and inefficiency create real ensemble opportunities, but the shorter data history and more extreme regime shifts demand even greater discipline. The practitioner who builds five genuinely uncorrelated, simple (3\u20135 parameter) strategies and runs them with equal weights will almost certainly outperform the practitioner who optimizes an elaborate weighted scoring system \u2014 not because weighting is theoretically inferior, but because the estimation error in determining good weights exceeds the theoretical benefit of having them.",

Default 2.\\n        fast_period (int): SMA fast period. Default 5 (tuned for 5m candles).\\n        slow_period (int): SMA slow period. Default 15.\\n        rsi_period (int): RSI lookback. Default 14.\\n        rsi_oversold (float): RSI buy threshold. Default 35.\\n        rsi_overbought (float): RSI sell threshold. Default 65.\\n        macd_fast (int): MACD fast EMA. Default 8.\\n        macd_slow (int): MACD slow EMA. Default 21.\\n        macd_signal (int): MACD signal period. Default 5.\\n        bb_period (int): Bollinger Bands period. Default 15.\\n        bb_std (float): Bollinger Bands std dev. Default 2.0.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n        super().__init__(params)\\n        self.min_agree = params.get(\\\"min_agree\\\", 2)\\n\\n        #       "file_text": "\"\"\"\nConsensus Strategy \u2014 runs ALL strategies, trades when any has a strong signal.\n\nEvidence-backed approach (Bates & Granger 1969, forecast combination literature):\n- Runs 4 diverse strategies (trend, momentum, volatility) every tick\n- Equal weights \u2014 research shows this beats complex optimization\n- Trades when ANY strategy signals with confidence above a threshold\n- More strategies agreeing = higher confidence = larger position sizing potential\n- Filters out weak/noisy signals via confidence_threshold\n\nThis produces frequent trades while avoiding low-quality signals.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pandas as pd\nimport structlog\n\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\nfrom agent.strategies.sma_crossover import SMACrossoverStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.rsi import RSIStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.macd import MACDStrategy\nfrom agent.strategies.bollinger_bands import BollingerBandsStrategy\n\nlog = structlog.get_logger()\n\n\nclass ConsensusStrategy(BaseStrategy):\n    \"\"\"\n    Meta-strategy: runs all sub-strategies, trades on strong individual signals.\n\n    Config params:\n        min_agree (int): Minimum strategies that must agree. Default 1.\n        confidence_threshold (float): Minimum confidence to act. Default 0.5.\n        fast_period (int): SMA fast period. Default 5.\n        slow_period (int): SMA slow period. Default 15.\n        rsi_period (int): RSI lookback. Default 14.\n        rsi_oversold (float): RSI buy threshold. Default 35.\n        rsi_overbought (float): RSI sell threshold. Default 65.\n        macd_fast (int): MACD fast EMA. Default 8.\n        macd_slow (int): MACD slow EMA. Default 21.\n        macd_signal (int): MACD signal period. Default 5.\n        bb_period (int): Bollinger Bands period. Default 15.\n        bb_std (float): Bollinger Bands std dev. Default 2.0.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n        super().__init__(params)\n        self.min_agree = params.get(\"min_agree\", 1)\n        self.confidence_threshold = params.get(\"confidence_threshold\", 0.5)\n\n        # Build sub-strategies \u2014 diverse by design (trend + momentum + volatility)\n        self._strategies: list[BaseStrategy] = [\n            SMACrossoverStrategy({\n                \"fast_period\": params.get(\"fast_period\", 5),\n                \"slow_period\": params.get(\"slow_period\", 15),\n            }),\n            RSIStrategy({\n                \"period\": params.get(\"rsi_period\", 14),\n                \"oversold\": params.get(\"rsi_oversold\", 35),\n                \"overbought\": params.get(\"rsi_overbought\", 65),\n            }),\n            MACDStrategy({\n                \"fast_period\": params.get(\"macd_fast\", 8),\n                \"slow_period\": params.get(\"macd_slow\", 21),\n                \"signal_period\": params.get(\"macd_signal\", 5),\n            }),\n            BollingerBandsStrategy({\n                \"period\": params.get(\"bb_period\", 15),\n                \"std_dev\": params.get(\"bb_std\", 2.0),\n            }),\n        ]\n\n    @property\n    def name(self) -> str:\n        return \"consensus\"\n\n    @property\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\n        return max(s.required_history for s in self._strategies) + 5\n\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\n        # Run every sub-strategy\n        results: list[TradeRecommendation] = []\n        for strategy in self._strategies:\n            try:\n                rec = strategy.evaluate(pair, candles)\n                results.append(rec)\n            except Exception as e:\n                log.warning(\"consensus.sub_strategy_error\", strategy=strategy.name, error=str(e))\n\n        if not results:\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\n                confidence=0.0,\n                reason=\"No sub-strategies returned results\",\n                metadata={},\n            )\n\n        # Collect votes \u2014 only count signals above the confidence threshold\n        strong_buys: list[tuple[str, float]] = []   # (strategy_name, confidence)\n        strong_sells: list[tuple[str, float]] = []\n        all_votes: list[str] = []\n\n        strategy_details: dict[str, Any] = {}\n\n        for i, rec in enumerate(results):\n            s_name = self._strategies[i].name if i < len(self._strategies) else f\"strategy_{i}\"\n            all_votes.append(f\"{s_name}={rec.signal.value}\")\n\n            strategy_details[s_name] = {\n                \"signal\": rec.signal.value,\n                \"confidence\": rec.confidence,\n                \"reason\": rec.reason,\n            }\n\n            # Only count strong signals (above confidence threshold)\n            if rec.signal == Signal.BUY and rec.confidence >= self.confidence_threshold:\n                strong_buys.append((s_name, rec.confidence))\n            elif rec.signal == Signal.SELL and rec.confidence >= self.confidence_threshold:\n                strong_sells.append((s_name, rec.confidence))\n\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\n            \"buy_count\": len(strong_buys),\n            \"sell_count\": len(strong_sells),\n            \"hold_count\": len(results) - len(strong_buys) - len(strong_sells),\n            \"min_agree\": self.min_agree,\n            \"confidence_threshold\": self.confidence_threshold,\n            \"votes\": \", \".join(all_votes),\n            \"strategies\": strategy_details,\n            \"price\": float(candles[\"close\"].iloc[-1]),\n        }\n\n        # BUY: enough strong buy signals\n        if len(strong_buys) >= self.min_agree:\n            # Equal-weight average of confident strategies (research-backed)\n            avg_confidence = sum(c for _, c in strong_buys) / len(strong_buys)\n            # Bonus for agreement: more strategies = stronger signal\n            agreement_ratio = len(strong_buys) / len(results)\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.7 + agreement_ratio * 0.3)\n\n            names = [n for n, _ in strong_buys]\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"BUY ({len(strong_buys)}/{len(results)} strong): {', '.join(names)} [{', '.join(all_votes)}]\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # SELL: enough strong sell signals\n        if len(strong_sells) >= self.min_agree:\n            avg_confidence = sum(c for _, c in strong_sells) / len(strong_sells)\n            agreement_ratio = len(strong_sells) / len(results)\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.7 + agreement_ratio * 0.3)\n\n            names = [n for n, _ in strong_sells]\n            return TradeRecommendation(\n                pair=pair,\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\n                reason=f\"SELL ({len(strong_sells)}/{len(results)} strong): {', '.join(names)} [{', '.join(all_votes)}]\",\n                metadata=metadata,\n            )\n\n        # No strong signals \u2014 hold\n        return TradeRecommendation(\n            pair=pair,\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\n            confidence=0.2,\n            reason=f\"No strong signals (threshold={self.confidence_threshold}): [{', '.join(all_votes)}]\",\n            metadata=metadata,\n        )\n",

      "json_block": "{\"language\": \"python\", \"code\": \"\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nConsensus Strategy \\u2014 runs ALL strategies, trades when any has a strong signal.\\n\\nEvidence-backed approach (Bates & Granger 1969, forecast combination literature):\\n- Runs 4 diverse strategies (trend, momentum, volatility) every tick\\n- Equal weights \\u2014 research shows this beats complex optimization\\n- Trades when ANY strategy signals with confidence above a threshold\\n- More strategies agreeing = higher confidence = larger position sizing potential\\n- Filters out weak/noisy signals via confidence_threshold\\n\\nThis produces frequent trades while avoiding low-quality signals.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nfrom typing import Any\\n\\nimport pandas as pd\\nimport structlog\\n\\nfrom agent.strategies.base import BaseStrategy, Signal, TradeRecommendation\\nfrom agent.strategies.sma_crossover import SMACrossoverStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.rsi import RSIStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.macd import MACDStrategy\\nfrom agent.strategies.bollinger_bands import BollingerBandsStrategy\\n\\nlog = structlog.get_logger()\\n\\n\\nclass ConsensusStrategy(BaseStrategy):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Meta-strategy: runs all sub-strategies, trades on strong individual signals.\\n\\n    Config params:\\n        min_agree (int): Minimum strategies that must agree. Default 1.\\n        confidence_threshold (float): Minimum confidence to act. Default 0.5.\\n        fast_period (int): SMA fast period. Default 5.\\n        slow_period (int): SMA slow period. Default 15.\\n        rsi_period (int): RSI lookback. Default 14.\\n        rsi_oversold (float): RSI buy threshold. Default 35.\\n        rsi_overbought (float): RSI sell threshold. Default 65.\\n        macd_fast (int): MACD fast EMA. Default 8.\\n        macd_slow (int): MACD slow EMA. Default 21.\\n        macd_signal (int): MACD signal period. Default 5.\\n        bb_period (int): Bollinger Bands period. Default 15.\\n        bb_std (float): Bollinger Bands std dev. Default 2.0.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(self, params: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\\n        super().__init__(params)\\n        self.min_agree = params.get(\\\"min_agree\\\", 1)\\n        self.confidence_threshold = params.get(\\\"confidence_threshold\\\", 0.5)\\n\\n        # Build sub-strategies \\u2014 diverse by design (trend + momentum + volatility)\\n        self._strategies: list[BaseStrategy] = [\\n            SMACrossoverStrategy({\\n                \\\"fast_period\\\": params.get(\\\"fast_period\\\", 5),\\n                \\\"slow_period\\\": params.get(\\\"slow_period\\\", 15),\\n            }),\\n            RSIStrategy({\\n                \\\"period\\\": params.get(\\\"rsi_period\\\", 14),\\n                \\\"oversold\\\": params.get(\\\"rsi_oversold\\\", 35),\\n                \\\"overbought\\\": params.get(\\\"rsi_overbought\\\", 65),\\n            }),\\n            MACDStrategy({\\n                \\\"fast_period\\\": params.get(\\\"macd_fast\\\", 8),\\n                \\\"slow_period\\\": params.get(\\\"macd_slow\\\", 21),\\n                \\\"signal_period\\\": params.get(\\\"macd_signal\\\", 5),\\n            }),\\n            BollingerBandsStrategy({\\n                \\\"period\\\": params.get(\\\"bb_period\\\", 15),\\n                \\\"std_dev\\\": params.get(\\\"bb_std\\\", 2.0),\\n            }),\\n        ]\\n\\n    @property\\n    def name(self) -> str:\\n        return \\\"consensus\\\"\\n\\n    @property\\n    def required_history(self) -> int:\\n        return max(s.required_history for s in self._strategies) + 5\\n\\n    def evaluate(self, pair: str, candles: pd.DataFrame) -> TradeRecommendation:\\n        # Run every sub-strategy\\n        results: list[TradeRecommendation] = []\\n        for strategy in self._strategies:\\n            try:\\n                rec = strategy.evaluate(pair, candles)\\n                results.append(rec)\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                log.warning(\\\"consensus.sub_strategy_error\\\", strategy=strategy.name, error=str(e))\\n\\n        if not results:\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.HOLD,\\n                confidence=0.0,\\n                reason=\\\"No sub-strategies returned results\\\",\\n                metadata={},\\n            )\\n\\n        # Collect votes \\u2014 only count signals above the confidence threshold\\n        strong_buys: list[tuple[str, float]] = []   # (strategy_name, confidence)\\n        strong_sells: list[tuple[str, float]] = []\\n        all_votes: list[str] = []\\n\\n        strategy_details: dict[str, Any] = {}\\n\\n        for i, rec in enumerate(results):\\n            s_name = self._strategies[i].name if i < len(self._strategies) else f\\\"strategy_{i}\\\"\\n            all_votes.append(f\\\"{s_name}={rec.signal.value}\\\")\\n\\n            strategy_details[s_name] = {\\n                \\\"signal\\\": rec.signal.value,\\n                \\\"confidence\\\": rec.confidence,\\n                \\\"reason\\\": rec.reason,\\n            }\\n\\n            # Only count strong signals (above confidence threshold)\\n            if rec.signal == Signal.BUY and rec.confidence >= self.confidence_threshold:\\n                strong_buys.append((s_name, rec.confidence))\\n            elif rec.signal == Signal.SELL and rec.confidence >= self.confidence_threshold:\\n                strong_sells.append((s_name, rec.confidence))\\n\\n        metadata: dict[str, Any] = {\\n            \\\"buy_count\\\": len(strong_buys),\\n            \\\"sell_count\\\": len(strong_sells),\\n            \\\"hold_count\\\": len(results) - len(strong_buys) - len(strong_sells),\\n            \\\"min_agree\\\": self.min_agree,\\n            \\\"confidence_threshold\\\": self.confidence_threshold,\\n            \\\"votes\\\": \\\", \\\".join(all_votes),\\n            \\\"strategies\\\": strategy_details,\\n            \\\"price\\\": float(candles[\\\"close\\\"].iloc[-1]),\\n        }\\n\\n        # BUY: enough strong buy signals\\n        if len(strong_buys) >= self.min_agree:\\n            # Equal-weight average of confident strategies (research-backed)\\n            avg_confidence = sum(c for _, c in strong_buys) / len(strong_buys)\\n            # Bonus for agreement: more strategies = stronger signal\\n            agreement_ratio = len(strong_buys) / len(results)\\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.7 + agreement_ratio * 0.3)\\n\\n            names = [n for n, _ in strong_buys]\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.BUY,\\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\\n                reason=f\\\"BUY ({len(strong_buys)}/{len(results)} strong): {', '.join(names)} [{', '.join(all_votes)}]\\\",\\n                metadata=metadata,\\n            )\\n\\n        # SELL: enough strong sell signals\\n        if len(strong_sells) >= self.min_agree:\\n            avg_confidence = sum(c for _, c in strong_sells) / len(strong_sells)\\n            agreement_ratio = len(strong_sells) / len(results)\\n            final_confidence = min(0.95, avg_confidence * 0.7 + agreement_ratio * 0.3)\\n\\n            names = [n for n, _ in strong_sells]\\n            return TradeRecommendation(\\n                pair=pair,\\n                signal=Signal.SELL,\\n                confidence=round(final_confidence, 2),\\n                reason=f\\\"SELL ({len(strong_sells)}/{len(results)} strong): {', '.join(names)} [{', '.join(all_votes)}]\\\",\\n                metadata=metadata,\\n            )\\n\\n        # No strong signals \\u2014 hold\\n        return TradeRecommendation(\\n            pair=pair,\\n            signal=Signal.HOLD,\\n            confidence=0.2,\\n            reason=f\\\"No strong signals (threshold={self.confidence_threshold}): [{', '.join(all_votes)}]\\\",\\n            metadata=metadata,\\n        )\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/crypto-trading-agent/src/agent/strategies/consensus.py\"}"

\"open\": prices,\n            \"high\": [p * 1.01 for p in prices],\n            \"low\": [p * 0.99 for p in prices],\n            \"close\": prices,\n            \"volume\": [1000.0] * n,\n        },\n        index=pd.date_range(\"2025-01-01\", periods=n, freq=\"5min\", tz=\"UTC\"),\n    )\n\n\ndef test_hold_on_flat_data():\n    \"\"\"Flat prices \u2192 no strong signals \u2192 HOLD.\"\"\"\n candles)\n    assert result.signal == Signal.HOLD\n    assert \"votes\" in result.metadata\n\n\ndef test_metadata_has_vote_counts():\n    \"\"\"Metadata should include vote counts and confidence threshold.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\"min_agree\": 1, \"confidence_threshold\": 0.5})\n    prices = [100.0] * 50\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    assert \"buy_count\" in result.metadata\n    assert \"sell_count\" in result.metadata\n    assert \"hold_count\" in result.metadata\n    assert \"confidence_threshold\" in result.metadata\n    total = result.metadata[\"buy_count\"] + result.metadata[\"sell_count\"] + result.metadata[\"hold_count\"]\n    assert total == 4\n\n\ndef test_crash_triggers_buy():\n    \"\"\"A massive price crash should produce at least one strong buy signal.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\n        \"min_agree\": 1,\n        \"confidence_threshold\": 0.5,\n        \"rsi_period\": 14,\n        \"rsi_oversold\": 35,\n        \"bb_period\": 15,\n        \"bb_std\": 2.0,\n    })\n    # Steady at 100, then crash to 70 \u2192 RSI + Bollinger should flag oversold\n    prices = [100.0] * 40 + [70.0]\n    candles = _make_candles(prices)\n    result = strategy.evaluate(\"BTC/USDT\", candles)\n\n    # With min_agree=1, even one produce at least one strong sell signal.\"\"\"\n    strategy = ConsensusStrategy(params={\n        \"min_agree\": 1,\n        \"confidence_threshold\": 0.5,\n        \"rsi_period\": 14,\n     "type": "text",
    "text": "Honest take based on the research we just did:\n\n**Running multiple strategies simultaneously with separate cash allocations \u2014 yes, that's well-supported.** That's essentially what hedge funds call a \"multi-strategy pod\" approach. Each strategy gets an equal slice of capital and trades independently. This is one of the strongest findings in the literature.\n\n**Dynamically moving funds between them based on recent performance \u2014 that's the trap.** The research was explicit about this: optimizing weights based on recent results almost always overfits. A strategy that performed well last week might be about to enter a market regime where it fails. Quantopian's 888-strategy study showed backtested performance had essentially zero predictive power for future performance.\n\n**What I'd recommend instead:**\n\nGive each strategy an equal, fixed 25% of capital and let them all trade independently. Don't shuffle money between them. If after a few months of paper trading you see one strategy is consistently terrible, you manually remove it \u2014 but the agent shouldn't be auto-rotating funds.\n\nThis is simpler to build, harder to overfit, and backed by the equal-weighting research. It would also make your dashboard much more interesting \u2014 you'd see 4 independent P&L curves and could compare real-time performance across strategies.\n\nWant me to build that \u2014 4 independent strategy pods each with $2,500, all trading simultaneously?",
